target.title: fix(exec): reuse duplicate background sessions
target.body:
## Summary

- Repeated identical scoped background-capable `exec` retries now reuse the existing running process session instead of launching another child process.
- Reuse covers explicit `background: true`, explicit `yieldMs`, and the default auto-yield path where the model omits both fields.
- The match is conservative: command, resolved cwd, scoped process identity, reuse mode, and resolved execution options must match.
- Plain foreground reruns, completed reruns, env/PTY/timeout/cwd/scope/session drift, and normal `process` polling behavior remain separate.

## AI assistance

This PR was implemented with Codex assistance. I reviewed the change and understand the affected `exec` / `process` runtime path.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed:
Identical scoped `exec` calls with omitted `background` and `yieldMs` reuse the running default auto-yield session instead of spawning duplicate processes.

Real environment tested:
Local OpenClaw source checkout on branch `fix-62432`, using the real `createExecTool` and `createProcessTool` implementations with isolated `OPENCLAW_HOME`, `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, and `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`. No live provider, channel, or credential state was used.

Exact steps or command run after this patch:

```sh
mkdir -p .artifacts/pr-proof
proof_log=".artifacts/pr-proof/62432-20260601T142833Z-default-auto-yield.log"
OPENCLAW_EXEC_SHELL_SNAPSHOT=0 \
OPENCLAW_HOME="$(mktemp -d)" \
OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR="$(mktemp -d)" \
OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH="$(mktemp -u)" \
node --import tsx --input-type=module <<'EOF' | tee "$proof_log"
import process from "node:process";
import { createExecTool, createProcessTool } from "./src/agents/bash-tools.js";
import { resetProcessRegistryForTests } from "./src/agents/bash-process-registry.js";

const scopeKey = "issue-62432-default-auto-yield-proof";
const command = "sleep 30";
resetProcessRegistryForTests();

const execTool = createExecTool({
  allowBackground: true,
  backgroundMs: 10,
  cwd: process.cwd(),
  notifyOnExit: false,
  scopeKey,
});
const processTool = createProcessTool({ scopeKey, cleanupMs: 60_000 });

const first = await execTool.execute("proof-default-yield-1", { command });
const second = await execTool.execute("proof-default-yield-2", { command });
const listed = await processTool.execute("proof-list", { action: "list" });
const runningSessions = (listed.details?.sessions ?? []).filter(
  (session) => session.status === "running",
);

console.log(`Evidence after fix: firstSessionId=${first.details?.sessionId}; secondSessionId=${second.details?.sessionId}; runningSessions=${runningSessions.length}`);
console.log(`Observed result after fix: reused=${first.details?.sessionId === second.details?.sessionId}; firstStatus=${first.details?.status}; secondStatus=${second.details?.status}`);

await processTool.execute("proof-remove", {
  action: "remove",
  sessionId: first.details?.sessionId,
});
resetProcessRegistryForTests();
EOF
```

Evidence after fix:

```txt
Behavior addressed: identical exec calls with omitted background/yieldMs reuse the running default auto-yield session
Real environment tested: local OpenClaw exec/process tools via node --import tsx, isolated OPENCLAW_HOME/STATE/CONFIG, scopeKey=issue-62432-default-auto-yield-proof
Exact steps or command run after this patch: createExecTool({backgroundMs:10, scopeKey}).execute({command:"sleep 30"}) twice, then process list
Evidence after fix: firstSessionId=young-mist; secondSessionId=young-mist; runningSessions=1
Observed result after fix: reused=true; firstStatus=running; secondStatus=running
What was not tested: provider UI rendering, remote gateway/node host, and explicit human approval prompts
First response: Command still running (session young-mist, pid 72708). Use process (list/poll/log/write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.
Second response: Command still running (session young-mist, pid 72708). Use process (list/poll/log/write/send-keys/submit/paste/kill/clear/remove) for follow-up.
Cleanup: Removed session young-mist (termination requested).
proof_log=.artifacts/pr-proof/62432-20260601T142833Z-default-auto-yield.log
```

Observed result after fix:
Both default auto-yield `exec` calls returned `status: "running"` with the same session id, and `process list` reported one running session.

What was not tested:
No live Xiaomi/MiMo replay or provider-driven agent turn was run. The proof exercises the runtime/tool path directly; GitHub CI covers the broader platform matrix.

## Root Cause

`handleExec` called `runExecProcess` before returning the running-session guidance. `runExecProcess` generated and registered a fresh process session for each call. The registry had id/list lookups, but no scoped same-execution lookup for an already-backgrounded in-flight process.

The issue is easiest to trigger when a model ignores the process follow-up guidance and repeats the same long-running `exec` call. Before this patch, OpenClaw treated that retry as a new launch.

## Dependency contract

External dependencies: none.

Source and docs checked:

- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts`
- `src/agents/bash-tools.process.ts`
- `docs/gateway/background-process.md`
- `docs/tools/exec.md`

Contract used here: backgrounded sessions are managed through the `process` tool, are scoped by process identity, and are only pollable/listed when backgrounded. This patch stays inside that registry/tool contract and does not change public tool schemas.

## Regression Test Plan

- Added registry coverage for exact command/cwd/execution-key/reuse-key scoped matches, missing execution identity, mismatched reuse mode, mismatched scope/session, different command, different cwd, and foreground sessions.
- Added behavior coverage for repeated identical scoped explicit-background `exec` calls.
- Added behavior coverage for repeated identical scoped default auto-yield `exec` calls with omitted `background` and `yieldMs`.
- Added coverage for overlapping explicit background retries while the first spawn is still in flight.
- Added negative coverage that env, PTY, and timeout drift start distinct sessions.
- Added negative coverage that a plain foreground rerun is not collapsed into an active explicit-background session.
- Added runtime cleanup coverage for pre-spawn setup failures after an explicit background session is registered.

## Security Impact

No new permissions, network calls, credential handling, or approval bypass. The duplicate-session check runs after the existing exec target/security/workdir/approval path. Execution identity is stored as an opaque SHA-256 hash, not raw environment data.

## Human Verification

- Reproduced the pre-fix runtime behavior with two direct `runExecProcess` calls: `before_fix_running_sessions=2`.
- Ran the after-fix runtime proof above and reviewed the exec/process path.
- Confirmed the branch was rebased onto the refreshed `upstream/main` after local `main` and fork `origin/main` were safely fast-forwarded.

## CODEOWNERS / owner review

Checked `.github/CODEOWNERS`; the touched `src/agents/bash-*` files do not match the restricted auth/sandbox/secops entries. Expected review is normal agent runtime maintainer review.

## Changelog

No contributor `CHANGELOG.md` edit.

## Review conversations

- Target issue search found no linked or exact open PR for `62432`.
- Related closed/stale PRs were checked for fix shape and maintainer intent.
- A codex-spark worker was asked to inspect/implement; its partial edits were reviewed and corrected before integration.
- ClawSweeper/local review found concrete issues during drafting: option-drift reuse, pre-spawn phantom sessions, foreground rerun collapse, and missing default auto-yield coverage. This patch addresses them with code and tests.
- Final `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` returned clean.
- Standalone `codex review --base origin/main` was run after the final rebase, but the command timed out after 30 minutes before returning a verdict.

## Risks

An explicit background/yield/default-yield retry that intentionally wants a second identical concurrent process in the same scoped process identity needs a distinct execution option or command text. Plain foreground reruns are preserved.

## Behavior tradeoffs

The guard uses exact execution identity and an explicit reuse-mode key instead of fuzzy command similarity. That keeps the fix narrow and avoids guessing intent from generated text.

## Scope boundaries

- No provider-specific Xiaomi/MiMo routing change.
- No loop-detection threshold or config change.
- No process persistence, schema, docs, or public config change.
- Completed-session reruns and plain foreground reruns remain allowed.
- Unscoped ad hoc exec calls are not coalesced.

## Validation

- `git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 src/agents/bash-process-registry.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec.ts src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts`
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts` -> 94 tests passed
- `OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/agents/bash-tools.exec-runtime.test.ts src/agents/bash-process-registry.test.ts src/agents/tool-loop-detection.test.ts src/agents/agent-tools.before-tool-call.e2e.test.ts` -> 3 Vitest shards passed
- `OPENCLAW_CHECK_CHANGED_REMOTE_CHILD=1 OPENCLAW_CHANGED_LANES_RAW_SYNC=1 OPENCLAW_HEAVY_CHECK_LOCK_SCOPE=worktree PNPM_CONFIG_VERIFY_DEPS_BEFORE_RUN=false pnpm check:changed`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode branch --base origin/main` -> clean
- `codex review --base origin/main` -> attempted; timed out after 30 minutes without a verdict
- Real runtime proof above

target.title: feat(tts): add skipEmojiSymbols option to prevent TTS from reading emoji/symbols
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the opt-in messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols TTS preprocessing setting.
- Applies emoji/symbol stripping through the shared speech-core preparation path before file, stream, and telephony synthesis requests.
- Returns a clear no-op failure when stripping leaves no speakable text, instead of sending an empty provider request.
- Keeps default behavior unchanged when the option is disabled or unset.
- Reviewer focus: config schema/docs, the shared TTS preprocessing path, and the empty-after-strip guard.

Out of scope: changing default TTS behavior, validating every vendor-specific pronunciation path, or adding provider-specific filtering.

## Linked context

- Related: #77969.
- This was opened as a focused external fix for noisy TTS output when emoji and decorative symbols are spoken by providers.
- No maintainer-only deployment, credential, or production environment change is required.

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: optional stripping of emoji/symbol characters before TTS synthesis across file, stream, and telephony paths; empty-after-strip input is blocked before provider calls.
- Real environment tested: local OpenClaw checkout on Linux, branch tts-skip-v3, commit 06545b841d08, using the bundled real Microsoft speech provider path (extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com). No API keys or private account secrets were used.
  - node --import tsx - <<'TS' ... register microsoft plugin, call textToSpeech twice with skipEmojiSymbols=false/true, then call an emoji-only enabled case ... TS
  - ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
  - node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
  - pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
- Evidence after fix:

      $ node --import tsx - <<'TS'
      # Registers the bundled Microsoft Speech plugin and performs real synthesis
      # through node-edge-tts / speech.platform.bing.com after this patch.
      TS
      {
        "branch": "tts-skip-v3",
        "commit": "06545b841d08",
        "provider": "microsoft",
        "realProviderPath": "extensions/microsoft -> node-edge-tts -> speech.platform.bing.com",
        "inputText": "Proof phrase: package ready. Check mark ✓ copyright © melting face 🫠 done.",
        "observed": [
          {
            "name": "disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": false,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/disabled-skipEmojiSymbols-false.mp3",
            "bytes": 39456,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          },
          {
            "name": "enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true",
            "skipEmojiSymbols": true,
            "success": true,
            "provider": "microsoft",
            "outputFormat": "audio-24khz-48kbitrate-mono-mp3",
            "target": "audio-file",
            "savedArtifact": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof/enabled-skipEmojiSymbols-true.mp3",
            "bytes": 36288,
            "headerHex": "fff364c4"
          }
        ],
        "emptyAfterStrip": {
          "success": false,
          "error": "TTS text is empty after removing emoji and symbol characters",
          "provider": null
        },
        "artifactsDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78172-real-proof"
      }

      $ ffprobe -v error -show_entries format=duration,size ...
      ffprobe was not installed in this container, so artifact validation used byte size plus MP3 frame header checks.

      $ ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       32 passed (32)

      $ node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
      Test Files  1 passed (1)
      Tests       14 passed (14)

      $ pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod
      exit_code=0

- Observed result after fix: disabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio from the original text containing ✓, ©, and 🫠; enabled mode generated real Microsoft speech audio after the shared speech-core filter removed those symbol/emoji characters; emoji/symbol-only enabled input failed before any provider was selected.
- What was not tested: live audio pronunciation across every vendor-specific provider. The real-provider proof uses the bundled Microsoft speech provider because it is credential-free and exercises the same speech-core preprocessing path that all providers share.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: ffprobe was not installed in the proof container, so artifact validation used nonzero byte sizes and MP3 frame headers. The live proof used the credential-free Microsoft path rather than paid or account-bound providers.
- Before evidence: not captured in this PR body; the regression was that emoji/symbol text reached synthesis without a shared opt-in strip step and empty-after-strip requests could still reach provider setup paths.

## Tests and validation

Commands run for the behavioral change:

    node --import tsx - <<'TS' ...real Microsoft speech-provider proof...
    ./node_modules/.bin/vitest run extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
    node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-tools.config.ts src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts
    pnpm check:test-types && pnpm tsgo:prod

Regression coverage added or updated:

- extensions/speech-core/src/tts.test.ts
- src/agents/tools/tts-tool.test.ts

Locked-in scenarios:

- file synthesis strips complete emoji/symbol sequences when enabled.
- stream synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- telephony synthesis receives stripped text when enabled.
- telephony synthesis does not call providers when stripping leaves empty text.
- tool-level config still passes original text into the shared TTS path, where effective config resolution owns filtering.

Template repair validation on 2026-05-27:

    Authenticated GitHub API read of the PR base template and PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API update of this PR body.
    Authenticated GitHub API re-read verified these current template headings are present:
    Summary, Linked context, Real behavior proof (required for external PRs), Tests and validation, Risk checklist, Current review state.

## Risk checklist

- User-visible behavior changed: Yes, but only when messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols is explicitly enabled.
- Config, environment, or migration behavior changed: Yes, a new optional config field is added; no migration is needed and unset/false preserves existing behavior.
- Security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior changed: No.
- Highest-risk area: Unicode filtering might remove characters a user expected to hear, or filtering might diverge between TTS paths.
- Mitigation: the option is disabled by default, filtering is centralized in the shared speech-core preparation path, and regression tests cover file, stream, telephony, empty-after-strip, and adjacent tool behavior.

Compatibility notes:

- Backward compatible: Yes.
- Config/env changes: Yes, optional messages.tts.skipEmojiSymbols.
- Migration needed: No.
- Upgrade steps: none unless a user wants to opt into emoji/symbol stripping.

## Current review state

- Next action: resolve the separate upstream/main generated config metadata conflicts for this branch, then let maintainers and automation continue review.
- Still waiting on author/maintainer/CI/external proof: author-side branch conflict refresh remains tracked separately; no additional external proof is being added in this body-only update.
- Bot or reviewer comments addressed: this body update repairs the current-template gap by replacing the older long-form PR body headings with every current upstream template heading and concrete content under each one.

target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `bf3ad1a9861ebe0ee4e0a90533d065cb69ae7201`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "bf3ad1a9861ebe0ee4e0a90533d065cb69ae7201",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-bf3ad1a986",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.

target.title: fix(auth-profiles): repair stale auto runtime auth selection
target.body:
## Summary

Control UI sessions could inherit a temporary fallback auth/model choice as if it were a real user choice.
That made a fresh WebChat/TUI session show or start from the fallback provider instead of the agent primary.
This change treats runtime-only auto auth fallback state as temporary, repairs it back to the configured primary, and keeps the auth resolver provider-scoped.

AI-assisted: yes.

Fixes #85126.

## What Changed

The fix stays inside core session/model selection.
It does not add config, plugin SDK, plugin runtime, manifest, or provider contract surface.

- Added an internal helper for the bad persisted shape: auto auth profile plus runtime provider/model, with no durable model override.
- Made `createModelSelectionState` switch that shape back to the configured primary and clear stale runtime/auth fields together.
- Made gateway model resolution ignore those stale runtime fields before dispatch/preflight code uses them.
- Stopped `sessions.create` from copying runtime-only auto fallback auth/model state from a parent into a fresh dashboard session.
- Added regressions for MiniMax primary plus DeepSeek runtime/auth fallback, gateway model resolution, and dashboard session creation.

## Testing

I tested the focused state repair path locally, then ran the changed type/lint/guard gate remotely.
The remote broad proof used AWS Crabbox because Blacksmith Testbox was unavailable in this environment (`blacksmith` CLI was missing).

- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --write --threads=1 src/sessions/model-overrides.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.ts src/gateway/session-utils.ts src/gateway/server-methods/sessions.ts src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/model-selection.test.ts src/gateway/session-utils.test.ts src/gateway/server.sessions.create.test.ts src/sessions/model-overrides.test.ts src/agents/auth-profiles/session-override.test.ts src/agents/agent-scope.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/auto-reply/reply/agent-runner-execution.test.ts -t "auto fallback"`
- `node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider aws --idle-timeout 90m --ttl 240m --timing-json --shell -- "pnpm check:changed"`

Behavior addressed: runtime-only auto fallback auth/model state no longer pins a session to the fallback provider when the configured primary should be selected.
Real environment tested: local macOS worktree with synthetic session-store regressions; AWS Crabbox Linux changed gate.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: the commands listed above.
Evidence after fix: focused Vitest regressions passed; `pnpm check:changed` passed on AWS Crabbox provider `aws`, lease `cbx_0417338a2dcb`, run `run_9b21f45d5a7d`, exit code 0.
Observed result after fix: a session with primary `minimax/MiniMax-M2.7`, runtime `deepseek/deepseek-v4-flash`, and auto `deepseek:default` auth resolves back to MiniMax and clears stale runtime/auth fields before the next dispatch.
What was not tested: live MiniMax/DeepSeek provider credentials and a forced real provider fallback were not used; the proof is synthetic plus type/lint/guard coverage.

## Risks

The behavior change is narrow and source-gated.
It only applies when the auth profile source is `auto`, an auth profile is present, and there is no persisted model override.

- User model overrides still win through `providerOverride`/`modelOverride`.
- User auth profiles are not treated as auto fallback state.
- Normal auto fallback overrides with persisted provider/model provenance still use the existing primary-probe path.

target.title: fix(matrix): suppress noisy cron tool-timeout alerts
target.body:
## Summary
- suppress user-facing cron failure notifications for the exact isolated-runner watchdog timeout `cron: job execution timed out (last phase: tool-execution-started)`
- preserve the cron run as an error with `lastError` intact
- mark suppressed failure-notification delivery as `not-requested` instead of leaving it ambiguous
- keep ordinary cron failures, generic timeouts, webhook destinations, and provider/model failures notifying normally

## Proof
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.gateway.config.ts src/gateway/server.cron.test.ts` — 20 passed
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.cron.config.ts src/cron/service.persists-delivered-status.test.ts` — 16 passed
- `git diff --check main...HEAD`
- `pnpm -s lint:core`
- `pnpm -s tsgo:core:test`

## Notes
This intentionally does not hide the cron error from run state. It only suppresses the noisy Matrix/user-facing failure alert for the narrow false-alarm timeout where the embedded agent was already in tool execution.

target.title: feat(plugins): upgrade llm_input/llm_output hooks from fire-and-forget to modifying
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: `llm_input` and `llm_output` plugin hooks are fire-and-forget (observe-only). Plugins cannot block, modify, or redact LLM calls/responses.
- Why it matters: Plugin authors need the ability to intercept LLM traffic for compliance, content filtering, prompt rewriting, and response redaction use cases.
- What changed: Both hooks now run sequentially via `runModifyingHook`. `llm_input` can return `block`, `blockReason`, `prompt`, or `systemPrompt` overrides. `llm_output` can return replacement `assistantTexts`. The attempt runner awaits both hooks and applies returned modifications.
- Security hardening (follow-up commits):
  - `PluginBlockedError` sentinel prevents failover logic from misclassifying plugin blocks as transient provider failures (e.g. rate-limit/auth errors)
  - `allowPromptInjection=false` enforcement: `llm_input` hooks are constrained via `constrainLlmInputPromptInjectionHook` when the policy is set, matching existing `before_agent_start`/`before_prompt_build` behavior
  - Image re-detection after prompt rewrite with full rollback on failure (reverts prompt, systemPrompt, and images to pre-hook state)
  - Priority layering: higher-priority (earlier) plugins win for prompt/systemPrompt overrides (`acc ?? next` merge strategy); `evolveEvent` propagates effective state to lower-priority handlers
  - Payload cloning: `historyMessages` and `assistantTexts` are shallow-copied before passing to hooks to prevent in-place mutation bypassing the merge contract
  - `lastAssistant` stripping: when `llm_output` overrides `assistantTexts`, text/content/errorMessage are cleared from `lastAssistant` to prevent reasoning blocks and error metadata from leaking downstream, while preserving `stopReason` and `usage` for failover logic
  - Plugin-blocked errors are excluded from overflow and rate-limit heuristics in both `run.ts` and `agent-runner-execution.ts`
- What did NOT change (scope boundary): Hook event shapes are unchanged (additive result types only). Plugins that return `void` behave identically to before. No changes to other hooks, plugin discovery, or the Plugin SDK public surface.

## Change Type (select all)

- [ ] Bug fix
- [x] Feature
- [ ] Refactor required for the fix
- [ ] Docs
- [x] Security hardening
- [ ] Chore/infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [ ] Auth / tokens
- [ ] Memory / storage
- [ ] Integrations
- [x] API / contracts
- [ ] UI / DX
- [ ] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #
- Related #57585 — adds a complementary `transform_context` hook at the context-window layer; this PR operates at the prompt-assembly/response-delivery layer instead
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause / Regression History (if applicable)

N/A

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

- Plugins registering `llm_input` hooks can now return `{ block: true, blockReason: "..." }` to prevent an LLM call from being made.
- Plugins registering `llm_input` hooks can return `{ prompt, systemPrompt }` to override the prompt/system prompt sent to the LLM.
- Plugins registering `llm_output` hooks can return `{ assistantTexts: [...] }` to replace the assistant response (e.g. for redaction).
- Existing plugins that return `void` from these hooks are unaffected — fully backward compatible.
- When `allowPromptInjection=false` is set for a plugin, `llm_input` prompt/systemPrompt fields are stripped from the hook result (block/blockReason still honored).

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[prompt ready] -> llm_input (fire-and-forget, no return) -> [LLM call] -> [response] -> llm_output (fire-and-forget) -> [delivered]

After:
[prompt ready] -> await llm_input -> [block? -> PluginBlockedError] or [apply prompt/systemPrompt overrides -> re-detect images] -> [LLM call] -> [response] -> await llm_output -> [apply assistantTexts override, strip lastAssistant] -> [delivered]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? Yes
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? No
- New/changed network calls? No
- Command/tool execution surface changed? No
- Data access scope changed? No
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: Plugins can now modify prompts and block/alter LLM responses. This is gated behind the existing plugin hook registration mechanism — only installed and enabled plugins can register these hooks. The blocking path throws a dedicated `PluginBlockedError` sentinel that is logged and excluded from failover/overflow misclassification. Prompt modifications are subject to `allowPromptInjection` policy enforcement. Hook payloads are cloned to prevent mutation. Response overrides strip original content from `lastAssistant` to prevent data leaks. Prompt/response modifications flow through the same trust boundary as existing modifying hooks (e.g. `before_tool_call`).

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: macOS
- Runtime/container: Node 22+ / Bun
- Model/provider: Any
- Integration/channel (if any): N/A
- Relevant config (redacted): Plugin with `llm_input`/`llm_output` hook registered

### Steps

1. Register a plugin with an `llm_input` hook that returns `{ block: true, blockReason: "test" }`
2. Trigger an agent run
3. Observe the LLM call is blocked with the logged reason

### Expected

- LLM call is blocked and error is thrown with reason

### Actual

- LLM call is blocked and error is thrown with reason

## Evidence

- [x] Code review hardening: all 12 review comments from automated reviewers (Codex/Greptile) addressed across 9 follow-up commits
- [ ] Failing test/log before + passing after
- [x] Trace/log snippets: manual gateway test with a local test plugin confirmed `llm_input` and `llm_output` hooks fire correctly on v2026.4.1 gateway. Logs show `llm_input fired` with prompt rewrite (`-> rewriting prompt (prepending tag)`), `llm_output fired` with `assistantTexts (1)` and usage data, and `-> BLOCKING: prompt contains "BLOCKED"` for the block path. The released gateway treats hooks as fire-and-forget (return values ignored), confirming backward compatibility; modifying behavior (block/rewrite/redact) is validated by 22 automated tests.
- [ ] Screenshot/recording
- [ ] Perf numbers (if relevant)

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: Hook blocking, prompt override, response redaction via test plugins, multi-plugin priority chaining with `evolveEvent` propagation
- Manual gateway testing: installed a local test plugin (`llm-hook-test`) on a live v2026.4.1 gateway via `openclaw plugins install -l`. Confirmed hook registration, `llm_input` firing with prompt data (provider, model, sessionId, imagesCount), `llm_output` firing with assistantTexts and usage. Tested block, rewrite, redact, and observe scenarios through the dashboard web UI. Verified the released gateway's fire-and-forget path is unaffected (backward compat).
- Automated tests: 22/22 tests pass in `wired-hooks-llm-modifying.test.ts` covering block result, prompt/systemPrompt override, evolveEvent propagation, multi-plugin priority merge, backward compat (void returns), and error handling (graceful degradation).
- Edge cases checked: Plugin returning void (backward compat), hook failure graceful degradation (logged warning, continues), `allowPromptInjection=false` constraint, image re-detection failure rollback, empty prompt override (`prompt: ""`), `PluginBlockedError` bypassing failover/overflow heuristics
- What you did **not** verify: End-to-end production load with many concurrent plugins across multiple providers

## Review Conversations

- [x] I replied to or resolved every bot review conversation I addressed in this PR.
- [x] I left unresolved only the conversations that still need reviewer or maintainer judgment.

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? Yes
- Config/env changes? No
- Migration needed? No

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: A misbehaving plugin could block all LLM calls or silently rewrite prompts.
  - Mitigation: Blocking is logged with reason via dedicated `PluginBlockedError`. Prompt/response modifications are opt-in (plugin must explicitly return override fields). `allowPromptInjection=false` can constrain prompt modification. Hook payloads are cloned. Existing hook failure handling catches and logs errors gracefully without crashing the run. Higher-priority plugins take precedence via first-setter-wins merge strategy.

target.title: refactor: migrate bundled transcript target lookups
target.body:
## What

Migrates bundled Discord and Telegram transcript target lookup paths onto the scoped transcript runtime APIs, while preserving current file-backed behavior and Telegram command `sessionFile` compatibility where that remains part of the command context.

## Why

This is part of the Path 3 SQLite branch-by-abstraction stack. These bundled callers were still deriving concrete transcript files from mutable session-store state, which blocked bundled-plugin SQLite validation from exercising storage-neutral transcript target APIs.

Refs #88838.

## Changes

- Read scoped transcript events
- Publish Telegram transcript updates
- Preserve command compatibility
- Add visible-text helper
- Update focused tests

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.ts` | Reads final assistant text from scoped transcript events |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.ts` | Uses scoped writer/publisher APIs for mirror writes |
| `extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.ts` | Resolves scoped target while preserving command `sessionFile` |
| `src/plugin-sdk/text-chunking.ts` | Exposes phase-aware assistant text extraction |
| `extensions/*/*.test.ts` | Updates Discord/Telegram regression coverage |

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/discord/src/monitor/message-handler.process.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-message-dispatch.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-transcript-hit.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/transcript-runtime-state.test.ts`

target.title: fix(imessage): replace node:readline with LF-only buffer to prevent U+2028 splitting JSON-RPC responses
target.body:
## Summary

Fixes #89830. Node `readline` treats U+2028 (LINE SEPARATOR) and U+2029 (PARAGRAPH SEPARATOR) as line terminators in addition to `\n`. When an incoming iMessage body contains raw U+2028 — which `imsg` emits verbatim inside JSON string values — a single valid JSON-RPC response is split into fragments that all fail `JSON.parse`. The pending RPC `id` is never resolved, and the poll loop re-fetches the same history window on every tick, producing a steady stream of parse errors and zero successful iMessage receives (head-of-line blocking).

## Fix

Replace `node:readline` `createInterface` in `IMessageRpcClient.start()` with manual stdout buffering that splits **only on `\n` (U+000A)**. This preserves the one-JSON-object-per-LF framing contract while keeping U+2028/U+2029 inside JSON string values intact.

Key changes in `extensions/imessage/src/client.ts`:
- Remove `node:readline` import and `Interface` member
- Add `stdoutBuffer` string field for chunk accumulation
- Use `child.stdout.on("data", ...)` with `indexOf("\n")` splitting instead of readline line events
- Clear buffer in `stop()` instead of closing reader

## Tests

New `extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts` (11 tests):
- **U+2028/U+2029 handling**: verifies LF-only splitting preserves JSON strings containing Unicode line/paragraph separators
- **LF framing**: multi-response splitting, cross-chunk buffering, empty line skipping
- **handleLine**: pending request resolution, error rejection, notification dispatch, unparseable input
- **Realistic scenario**: simulates imsg `messages.history` response with U+2028 in message text and verifies the pending request resolves correctly

All 23 iMessage tests pass (11 new + 12 existing).

## Validation

```
# Tests
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-imessage.config.ts extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts
→ 11 passed

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.extension-imessage.config.ts extensions/imessage/src/status.test.ts
→ 12 passed

# Lint
npx oxlint extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts
→ 0 warnings, 0 errors

# Type check
npx tsc --noEmit extensions/imessage/src/client.test.ts
→ no errors
```

target.title: refactor: add SQLite session store foundation
target.body:
## What

Path 3 / PR 3.2 for #88838 adds the unused, additive SQLite session/transcript store foundation behind the 3.1a accessor seam. It introduces the per-agent SQLite schema, generated Kysely types, SQLite-backed session/transcript accessor helpers, and shared conformance coverage while preserving the current file-backed runtime behavior.

## Why

The SQLite storage flip should not land in the same PR as caller rewrites or legacy migration work. This foundation lets the disposable validation branches exercise the future SQLite backend against the existing accessor seam, while production runtime remains file-backed until later Path 3 slices complete the caller adoption, doctor migration, and atomic storage flip.

## Changes

- Added additive per-agent SQLite schema/store modules
- Added file/SQLite accessor conformance tests
- Added transcript read-scope SQLite support
- Added canonical `sessions.json` path resolution parity
- Added `preserveActivity` parity for SQLite patches
- Serialized SQLite entry writes and transcript idempotency
- Added exact-key SQLite session lookup
- Excluded runtime storage wiring and migration work

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/state/openclaw-agent-schema.sql` | Adds session/transcript tables |
| `src/state/openclaw-agent-db.ts` | Adds per-agent SQLite DB helper |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.sqlite.ts` | Implements additive SQLite accessor helpers |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts` | Shares file/SQLite behavior coverage |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts` | Carries canonical exact-key seam dependency |

Included scope: additive SQLite session/transcript store foundation, transcript read-scope support, path-resolution parity, `preserveActivity` parity, serialized SQLite writes/idempotency fixes, and exact-key SQLite lookup.

Excluded scope: transcript identity/state adapter, transcript delete/existence APIs, branch/successor mutation APIs, compaction checkpoint SQLite APIs, production storage flip/runtime wiring, doctor migration, and runtime dual-read/fallback. Follow-up blocker: `clawdbot-d02.1.9.1.21` for the SQLite transcript identity/state adapter.

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.conformance.test.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts` passed, including exact-key proof from `91a33a9bea`
- `node scripts/generate-kysely-types.mjs --verify` passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core` passed
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test` passed
- `git diff --check` passed
- `pnpm build` passed
- `autoreview --mode local` returned clean after the false-success unchecked duplicate append issue was fixed

target.title: refactor: add session accessor seam
target.body:
## Summary

What problem does this PR solve?

- Adds the first additive slice for #88838: a file-backed session/transcript accessor seam that can later be implemented by SQLite without changing caller-facing domain shapes.
- Keeps runtime storage behavior unchanged: the new seam delegates to the current file-backed session store and transcript JSONL helpers.
- Adds a raw transcript event append helper that uses the existing transcript write lock and FIFO, so the seam does not introduce an unsafe write path.
- Intentionally out of scope: SQLite schema/store modules, runtime storage flip, doctor import, broad caller migration, and plugin SDK behavior changes.
- Success means reviewers can validate the seam shape and safety rules before later PRs use it as the SQLite migration boundary.
- Review focus: seam shape, transcript locking/path resolution, normalized session-key handling, and whether the additive boundary is small enough for the first migration slice.

Why does this matter now?

The core session/transcript SQLite migration is broad because many callers currently depend on file-era shapes. This creates a stable internal boundary first so storage changes and caller modernization can land separately.

What is the intended outcome?

A behavior-neutral internal seam over current file storage that later SQLite store modules can satisfy.

What is intentionally out of scope?

SQLite schema/store modules, runtime storage flip, doctor migration, and subsystem caller modernization.

What does success look like?

The seam is additive, tested against file storage behavior, and safe to build subsequent migration PRs on.

What should reviewers focus on?

Transcript write locking, topic/session path resolution, normalized session keys, metadata-only insert behavior, and whether the seam avoids runtime dual-read/fallback behavior.

## Linked context

Which issue does this close?

Does not close an issue.

Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related?

Refs #88838.
Related #79902.

Was this requested by a maintainer or owner?

Yes. This follows Josh's Path 3 SQLite decomposition plan for the core session/transcript migration.

## Path 3 implementation map

This PR is the PR 3.1a base seam for the broader #88838 SQLite migration. The current open PRs in the stack are:

| Section | PR | Status | Notes |
|---|---|---|---|
| PR 3.1a: canonical accessor seam | #88840 | Open | This PR; base for the 3.1b caller slices. |
| PR 3.1b: gateway entry/list/describe/get | #89120 | Open | Routes gateway session entry reads through the seam. |
| PR 3.1b: transcript readers | #89121 | Open | Routes storage-neutral transcript readers through the facade. |
| PR 3.1b: cron/infra/commands | #89122 | Open | Routes command/status/health read paths through the seam; 3.2 writer race remains tracked outside this PR. |
| PR 3.1b: transcript writers | #89123 | Open | Routes core transcript writer helpers through the seam. |
| PR 3.1b: auto-reply/agent runtime | #89124 | Open | Routes agent/runtime session writes through the seam. |
| PR 3.1b: plugin SDK compatibility | Not open yet | In validation/backport | Patch/preserveActivity and SQLite resolver findings are still being backported before PR creation. |
| PR 3.1b: bundled plugin consumers | Not open yet | In validation/backport | Narrow bundled plugin callers are being moved off whole-store mutation; remaining whole-store compatibility is a design item. |
| PR 3.2: SQLite store foundation | Not open yet | In blocker resolution | Additive SQLite foundation exists on a branch, with path parity and writer race fixes still being finalized. |
| Storage flip / doctor migration | Not open yet | Future phase | Explicitly out of scope for this PR and the current 3.1b caller slices. |

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

- Behavior or issue addressed: Adds an internal file-backed accessor seam for the core session/transcript SQLite migration plan.
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout in a Codex worktree on macOS.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch:
  - `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts`
  - `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts`
  - `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core`
  - `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
  - `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/prompts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`
- Evidence after fix: Focused Vitest passed 2 files / 32 tests; oxfmt, oxlint, core/test typechecks passed; autoreview reported no accepted/actionable findings.
- Observed result after fix: Session accessor reads/writes file-backed entries, creates durable session ids for metadata-only inserts, preserves topic transcript paths, persists normalized session keys, creates transcript files with 0600 mode, and appends raw transcript events through the existing lock/FIFO.
- What was not tested: Full runtime session migration, SQLite-backed implementation, doctor import, broad gateway/agent/SDK caller migration, and live end-to-end OpenClaw runs.
- Proof limitations or environment constraints: This PR is intentionally additive and does not flip storage behavior.
- Before evidence (optional but encouraged): Existing runtime used direct file-store/transcript helper paths without a dedicated storage-agnostic seam for the migration.

## Tests and validation

Which commands did you run?

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript.test.ts`
- `pnpm exec oxfmt --check src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts`
- `node scripts/run-oxlint.mjs src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts src/config/sessions/transcript-append.ts`
- `pnpm tsgo:core`
- `pnpm tsgo:core:test`
- `/Users/phaedrus/Projects/prompts/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

What regression coverage was added or updated?

Added `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.test.ts` covering file-backed load/list/upsert, metadata-only insert ids, transcript event load/append, secure transcript file creation, topic fallback paths, and normalized session-key persistence.

What failed before this fix, if known?

Autoreview caught several seam risks before this PR was opened: unsafe fallback session ids, insecure raw transcript file creation, missing topic fallback handling, cache mutation outside the writer, non-canonical session-key persistence, and raw appends bypassing the transcript lock/FIFO. Those were fixed before opening this PR.

If no test was added, why not?

Tests were added.

## Risk checklist

Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`)

No.

What is the highest-risk area?

The new raw transcript event append helper could become a reusable write path, so it must preserve existing transcript locking and file-permission behavior.

How is that risk mitigated?

The helper routes through the existing transcript append FIFO and session write lock, creates new files with `0600`, and has focused regression coverage.

## Current review state

What is the next action?

Maintainer review of the additive seam shape and safety constraints.

What is still waiting on author, maintainer, CI, or external proof?

CI and maintainer review. The broader migration remains tracked in #88838.

Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed?

No PR comments yet. Local autoreview findings were addressed before PR creation.

target.title: feat(browser): extend --labels overlay to full-page and element captures
target.body: Bring extensions/browser `--labels` screenshot to its full intended scope: full-page captures, element/ref captures, and a per-ref bounding-box metadata array — while preserving the shipped `labelsSkipped` response contract. - `--labels --full-page` now actually projects labels across the entire page (previously silently dropped fullPage in the Playwright path). - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` now clip the capture and project labels relative to the element top-left (previously ref/element were silently dropped in the labels path). - Response includes a new `annotations` array of per-ref bounding boxes (`{ref, number, role, name?, box: {x,y,width,height}}`) so agents can reason about positions without re-snapshotting. The field is omitted when empty. - `labelsSkipped` keeps its shipped meaning: in viewport capture, refs outside the captured viewport are counted as skipped (not silently re-interpreted). `labelsCount` reports the labels actually drawn on the image; `annotations` is an additive superset that also includes off-viewport refs with their boxes. Implementation: - New `screenshot-annotate.ts` pure helper: `refToNumber`, `planAnnotations` with viewport/fullpage/element coordinate projection (with overlap filter for element mode), `buildOverlayInjectionScript` and `buildOverlayClearScript` IIFE generators. Zero Playwright dependency so the helper is reusable for the raw-CDP path in a follow-up. - `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` rewritten to consume the helper. It reads scroll + viewport size in one `page.evaluate`; in viewport capture mode, refs whose document box falls outside the viewport rect are counted as skipped and surfaced in `annotations` but not drawn (and do not consume the label budget). `labels` returns the drawn overlay count. - `/screenshot` Playwright branch forwards fullPage/ref/element through to the helper and returns annotations. - `/snapshot` Playwright branch returns annotations only (snapshot remains intentionally viewport-only and does not gain new params). - LLM-facing safeDetails surfaces annotations to the agent. Behavior summary: - `labelsSkipped` (viewport): preserved — off-viewport refs raise the count, same as shipped `main`. `labelsCount` (viewport): drawn-in-viewport labels (matches shipped `main`). New `annotations` array is additive. - Existing fullPage + (ref|element) 400 validation is unchanged. chrome-mcp and raw-CDP paths are deliberately unchanged in this change. existing-session profiles continue to omit the `annotations` field. That is documented as a known downgrade and tracked for follow-up. Tests: - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` (31 cases): `refToNumber` forms; three coordinate-space projections; element-mode overlap filter; maxLabels truncation; empty-name omission; IIFE script content/idempotency; and viewport off-screen accounting (off-viewport counted as skipped, still surfaced in annotations, only when viewport size is supplied). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` (7 cases): all three capture modes, finally-cleanup-on-error, off-viewport-counted-as-skipped, and bbox-failure accounting. - All existing extensions/browser tests still pass. ## Review follow-ups (ClawSweeper) This revision addresses both findings from the review: - **[P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics** — Resolved by preserving the shipped contract instead of narrowing it. In viewport capture, off-viewport refs are counted as skipped again (matching `main`); `labelsCount` reports drawn-in-viewport labels; the new `annotations` array is purely additive (it includes off-viewport refs with their boxes so agents can still locate them). No existing consumer of `labelsSkipped` is silently re-interpreted, so no maintainer compatibility decision is required. - **[P2] raw-CDP labels caveat** — Resolved. The browser-automation skill now states that the raw-CDP fallback (no Playwright) does not support labeled screenshots at all (returns 501); only chrome-mcp/existing-session renders the overlay without `annotations`. ## Summary What problem does this PR solve? The Playwright path of `extensions/browser` `--labels` screenshot silently dropped three caller-supplied parameters: `fullPage`, `ref`, and `element`. A user passing `--labels --full-page` got a viewport-only capture with no error. A user passing `--labels --ref e3` got a viewport capture of the whole page, ignoring the element scoping. The labels response also gave the LLM no way to reason about a ref's on-image position without re-running snapshot, so multimodal agents had to re-snapshot for every layout question. Why does this matter now? The `--labels` flag is the workhorse for vision-capable agents working on long pages or single components. The silent-drop made any prompt-engineered combination such as "screenshot the form with labels" produce wrong-but-not-erroring output, which is a worse failure mode than a clear 4xx. Adding the `annotations` array is the single biggest information-density gain for that prompt path: positions become a few hundred bytes of structured JSON instead of a re-issued snapshot RPC. What is the intended outcome? - `--labels --full-page` produces a full-page PNG/JPG with labels in document coordinates, all the way to the bottom of the page. - `--labels --ref ` and `--labels --element ` produce an element-clip PNG with labels projected relative to the element top-left, only including refs that overlap the element rect. - Every Playwright-path labels response includes `annotations: [{ref, number, role, name?, box}]` (omitted when empty). - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures; `annotations` is a new additive field. - A pure helper (`screenshot-annotate.ts`) holds all coordinate math and DOM-injection IIFE strings, so the same logic can be reused by the raw-CDP path later without refactor. What is intentionally out of scope? - chrome-mcp path. `existing-session` profiles continue to use `renderChromeMcpLabels` and continue to omit the `annotations` field; this is a documented downgrade. - Raw-CDP fallback. Labels require Playwright; without it the route returns 501 (was already the case). - The `snapshot` route does not gain `fullPage / ref / element` parameters; the accompanying screenshot stays viewport-only. - No new CLI flag. `--labels` keeps its current shape; only the help text and the response payload were updated. - No `CHANGELOG.md` edits (per CONTRIBUTING.md). What does success look like? - Full page (`https://playwright.dev/`, ~4121px tall) with `--labels --full-page`: every interactive ref is drawn and annotated in document coordinates, `labelsSkipped: 0`, and after capture `document.querySelectorAll('[data-openclaw-labels]').length === 0` (no DOM residue). - Same page with viewport `--labels`: only refs inside the viewport are drawn; refs below the fold are counted in `labelsSkipped` (preserving the shipped contract) and still listed in `annotations` so an agent can scroll to them. What should reviewers focus on? - Coordinate-space correctness: `planAnnotations` projects `box` relative to viewport scroll for `viewport`, returns doc-space for `fullpage`, and projects relative to `elementRect` (with an overlap filter) for `element`. Tests in `screenshot-annotate.test.ts` lock each axis. - `labelsSkipped` preservation: in viewport mode an off-viewport ref raises `labelsSkipped`, is not drawn, and is still present in `annotations`. `labelsCount` counts drawn labels. `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` stays disjoint and equals the total resolved refs, matching shipped `main`. - Cleanup invariant: `buildOverlayClearScript()` MUST run even when `page.screenshot()` throws. Covered by `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` with a screenshot mock that throws. ## Linked context Which issue does this close? Closes # (No tracking issue exists. The behavior gaps were discovered while building a multimodal browser agent prompt and the `--labels` family is the natural seam for the fix.) Which issues, PRs, or discussions are related? Related # (None I am aware of. If a maintainer points to one I will link it.) Was this requested by a maintainer or owner? No. Drive-by contribution. The change is self-contained: deleting the new helper file and reverting `screenshotWithLabelsViaPlaywright` removes the feature cleanly. ## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs) - Behavior or issue addressed: - `--labels --full-page` was producing viewport-only output (silently dropping `fullPage`). - `--labels --ref ` / `--labels --element ` were producing whole-page viewport output (silently dropping the scope). - `--labels` responses had no per-ref bounding box data; agents had to re-snapshot to learn positions. - Review follow-up: `labelsSkipped` must keep its shipped meaning (off-viewport refs counted as skipped) rather than being narrowed. - Real environment tested: - OS: Linux 6.8.0-101-generic x86_64 - Node: v24.16.0 - Browser: headless Chromium (Playwright-bundled), driver `openclaw`, `cdpPort 9222` - Profile: `--profile dev` running `pnpm dev --profile dev gateway run` (source mode, freshly-built code paths) - Target page: `https://playwright.dev/` (~4121px tall scrollable document) - Head: `88942b10975aa5ee980cf57213f94f5af3ce2115` (current HEAD; all evidence below was re-captured against this SHA after rebasing on latest `main`) - Exact steps or command run after this patch: ``` alias oc='node openclaw.mjs --profile dev' oc browser open https://playwright.dev --label pa # Case 1: snapshot with annotations (viewport-only accompanying screenshot) oc browser --json snapshot --target-id pa --interactive --labels # Case 2: viewport screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels # Case 3: full-page screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --full-page # Case 4: element-clip screenshot with annotations oc browser --json screenshot pa --labels --element "nav" ``` - Evidence after fix (live JSON summaries, copied from the runs at HEAD `88942b1097`): | # | Command | labelsCount | labelsSkipped | annotations | max box.y | |---|---|---:|---:|---:|---:| | 1 | snapshot --interactive --labels (viewport) | 11 | 32 | 43 | 4121 | | 2 | screenshot --labels (viewport) | 13 | 51 | 64 | 4121 | | 3 | screenshot --labels --full-page | 64 | 0 | 64 | 1928 | | 4 | screenshot --labels --element "nav" | 4 | 0 | 4 | 15 | Reading of the numbers: - **`labelsSkipped` is preserved**: viewport captures (rows 1, 2) report 32 and 51 off-viewport refs as skipped — exactly the shipped behavior the review asked to keep. The pre-fix revision of this PR reported `labelsSkipped: 0` here. - **`annotations` is additive**: rows 1, 2 still surface all 43 / 64 resolved refs (including off-viewport ones, whose `box.y` reaches 4121 — outside the viewport image), so an agent can locate and scroll to them. - **Disjoint and consistent**: `labelsCount + labelsSkipped` equals the resolved-ref total in every row (11+32=43, 13+51=64, 64+0=64, 4+0=4). - **Full-page (row 3)**: the whole document is captured, so nothing is off-viewport: `labelsSkipped: 0`, all 64 drawn, and `max(box.y)=1928 ≤ resized image height (~2000)`. Visual captures from `https://playwright.dev/` (overlay/clipping behavior, unchanged by the `labelsSkipped` fix): **Viewport mode** (`screenshot --labels`): **Full-page mode** (`screenshot --labels --full-page`) — labels span the entire scrollable document: **Element-clip mode** (`screenshot --labels --element/--ref`) — capture clipped to the element rect; labels projected relative to the element top-left: - What was not tested: - chrome-mcp path on `existing-session` profile. Code review confirms the labels branch routes to `renderChromeMcpLabels` first; `annotations` is intentionally absent there. - Raw-CDP fallback. Code review confirms `--labels` requires Playwright and returns 501 otherwise. - DOM cleanup was not re-captured live this round (the `evaluate` CLI rejects a label-form `--target-id` in this flow); the `finally`-clear invariant is locked by the `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts` "runs the clear script even when screenshot throws" case. - macOS / Windows. Linux only; no OS-specific code paths touched. - Proof limitations or environment constraints: - Headless mode only. Headed-mode behavior should be identical (same `page.screenshot()` / `locator.screenshot()` calls). - Before evidence: - Before this fix on `main`, `--labels --full-page` produced a viewport-only PNG with no labels below the fold, and `--labels --ref/--element` ignored the scope. The response had no `annotations` field. The pre-fix revision of this PR additionally reported `labelsSkipped: 0` for viewport captures (the narrowing this revision reverts). ## Tests and validation Which commands did you run? ``` pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/screenshot-annotate.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts pnpm test extensions/browser/src/browser/client.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser/routes/agent.existing-session.test.ts extensions/browser/src/browser-tool.test.ts pnpm tsgo:extensions pnpm check:changed --base upstream/main ``` All green. The two annotate suites report `38 passed`; the labels-touching route/tool suites report `92 passed`. `check:changed` (typecheck extensions + extension tests + extension lint + guards) passes against latest `main`. What regression coverage was added or updated? - `screenshot-annotate.test.ts`: added a "viewport off-screen accounting" group — off-viewport refs are counted as skipped and still surfaced in `annotations` when viewport size is supplied, and not counted when it is omitted (pure-helper default). - `pw-tools-core.annotate.test.ts`: the former "off-viewport not filtered" case is now "off-viewport counted as skipped but still surfaced in annotations" (asserts `skipped`, `labels`, and `annotations`). ## Risk checklist Did user-visible behavior change? (`Yes/No`) Yes, additive and compatibility-preserving: - `--labels --full-page` now produces full-page output (was silently viewport-only). - `--labels --ref`/`--element` now produce element-clip output (was silently viewport-only). - New `annotations` array on Playwright-path labels responses. - `labelsSkipped` and `labelsCount` keep their shipped meaning for viewport captures (off-viewport refs counted as skipped; drawn count reported), so existing consumers are not silently re-interpreted. Did config, environment, or migration behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No config schema changes, no migrations, no new env vars, no plugin SDK surface changes. Did security, auth, secrets, network, or tool execution behavior change? (`Yes/No`) No. No new permissions, no SSRF policy changes, no new network destinations, no secret-surface changes, no `evaluate`/`act` changes. What is the highest-risk area? Coordinate-space invariants in `planAnnotations` (viewport vs fullpage vs element). A bug means agents reason about the wrong screen pixel. Mitigated by the three projection tests plus the live proof above. Secondary risk — DOM residue if cleanup fails — is mitigated by the unconditional `finally { clearScript }` block and the cleanup-on-error test. ## Current review state What is the next action? Maintainer review. Which bot or reviewer comments were addressed? - ClawSweeper [P1] `labelsSkipped` semantics: addressed by preserving the shipped contract (see Review follow-ups + Real behavior proof rows 1-3). - ClawSweeper [P2] raw-CDP labels caveat: addressed in `extensions/browser/skills/browser-automation/SKILL.md`.

target.title: fix(tui): show connection host in footer
target.body:
## Summary

- Show the URL-backed connection hostname in the TUI footer without exposing credentials, paths, or query strings.
- Skip non-URL local connection labels so embedded local mode does not invent a host.
- Update TUI docs and cover the formatter plus PTY footer rendering path.

Fixes #56276

## Verification

- `pnpm docs:list`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/tui/tui-formatters.test.ts src/tui/tui.test.ts src/tui/gateway-chat.test.ts`
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.tui-pty.config.ts`
- `git diff --check`
- `.agents/skills/autoreview/scripts/autoreview --mode local`

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: The TUI footer shows the hostname from a URL-backed Gateway connection, without showing the full URL, credentials, path, or query string.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw source checkout on macOS, running a real foreground OpenClaw Gateway with an isolated temporary OpenClaw config/state directory, then connecting the real TUI to that Gateway over loopback WebSocket.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the Gateway with `pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --auth none --bind loopback --port 18765 --ws-log compact`, waited for `[gateway] ready`, then connected the TUI with `pnpm openclaw tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18765 --token [redacted] --history-limit 1`.

Evidence after fix: Copied console output from the real Gateway + TUI run:

```text
$ pnpm openclaw gateway run --dev --auth none --bind loopback --port 18765 --ws-log compact
00:47:59 Dev config ready: /tmp/openclaw-real-proof.l6gVyf/openclaw.json
00:48:00 [gateway] http server listening (9 plugins: acpx, bonjour, browser, canvas, device-pair, file-transfer, memory-core, phone-control, talk-voice; 1.2s)
00:48:01 [gateway] ready

$ pnpm openclaw tui --url ws://127.0.0.1:18765 --token [redacted] --history-limit 1
openclaw tui - ws://127.0.0.1:18765 - agent dev (C3-PO) - session main
gateway connected | idle
host 127.0.0.1 | agent dev (C3-PO) | session main | openai/gpt-5.5 | think medium | tokens ?/200k
```

Observed result after fix: The TUI connected to the live local Gateway and the footer displayed `host 127.0.0.1` ahead of the agent/session/model fields. The footer did not display the token value, path, or query string.

What was not tested: A remote-machine or LAN Gateway was not used; this proof uses a loopback Gateway because the changed behavior formats the hostname from the TUI connection URL.

target.title: fix(googlechat): fall back to text link when remote media upload fails with 403
target.body:
### Summary

- **Problem**: Issue #89430 reports that Google Chat agents using app-auth (service-account, `chat.bot` scope) cannot deliver replies that include image or file attachments. The official Google Chat API contract requires user auth for `media.upload`; app-auth receives a 403. Both the outbound adapter and the monitor reply delivery path attempted the upload, propagated or dropped the 403, and silently discarded the agent reply.
- **Root Cause**: Two delivery paths unconditionally call `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` with no fallback. `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` wrapped the entire upload + `sendGoogleChatMessage` chain in one try/catch and logged the error without sending anything. `channel.adapters.ts` propagated the upload error up without any catch. `withGoogleChatResponse` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` for HTTP 403 on the upload endpoint, providing a precise error prefix that can distinguish upload auth failures from other 403s.
- **Fix**: Add `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` (matches the literal prefix `"Google Chat upload 403:"` to avoid false positives from `sendGoogleChatMessage` 403s that use `"Google Chat API 403:"`) and apply a narrow try/catch around only the `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` call in both paths. When upload fails with this error and the media URL is a remote http(s) URL, fall back to sending the URL as a text link with the caption. Non-403 upload failures still surface as errors; `sendGoogleChatMessage` stays outside the fallback scope in both paths.
- **What changed**:
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.adapters.ts` — wrap only `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` in try/catch; on 403 + remote URL send text link fallback; add `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` helper.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor-reply-delivery.ts` — restructure `sendMedia` to narrow the try/catch to the upload call only; same 403 fallback; add `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` helper.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts` — 2 new tests: 403 fallback returns text-link result, non-403 rethrows.
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 3 new tests: 403 with caption, 403 URL-only, non-403 still logs error.
- **What did NOT change (scope boundary)**:
  - `extensions/googlechat/src/actions.ts` — the ClawSweeper review named this as a third path; inspection shows it delegates through the same `channel.adapters.ts` outbound adapter, so the adapter fix covers it without a separate change.
  - Upload behavior for non-403 errors (network, size, other HTTP status) is unchanged.
  - Config surface unchanged (no schema, defaults, doctor migrations, or `docs/reference/config`).
  - Plugin SDK surface unchanged.
  - Gateway protocol unchanged.

### Reproduction

1. Configure Google Chat with service-account app auth (`chat.bot` scope).
2. Send a message that includes a remote image or file URL as a media attachment.
3. **Before this PR**: `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` receives a 403; `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` logs `"Google Chat attachment send failed"` and drops the reply; `channel.adapters.ts` propagates the error to the caller — no message is delivered.
4. **After this PR**: upload fails with `"Google Chat upload 403: …"`; the plugin detects it as a scope-gate failure; falls back to `sendGoogleChatMessage` with `[caption, mediaUrl].join("\n")` as text — the reply reaches the user as a link.

### Real behavior proof

**Behavior addressed (#89430)**: when `uploadGoogleChatAttachment` throws `"Google Chat upload 403: …"` and the media URL is a remote http(s) URL, both `channel.adapters.ts` and `monitor-reply-delivery.ts` now send a text-link fallback instead of dropping the reply.

**Real environment tested (Ubuntu Server Node 24, source-level — Vitest against production `deliverGoogleChatReply` and `googlechatOutboundAdapter.attachedResults.sendMedia` with mocked `uploadGoogleChatAttachment`)**: the 403 fallback tests call the production delivery functions with the mocked upload throwing the exact error format from `withGoogleChatResponse`, then assert on the `sendGoogleChatMessage` call args and status sink invocation. No live Google Chat credentials were available; the API contract (403 for `chat.bot` scope on `media.upload`) is documented and source-confirmed via `extensions/googlechat/src/api.ts:59` and `extensions/googlechat/src/auth.ts:11`.

**Exact steps or command run after this patch**: `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs extensions/googlechat/src/channel.test.ts extensions/googlechat/src/monitor.reply-delivery.test.ts` — 25 tests pass (20 pre-existing + 5 new). `pnpm exec oxlint` clean. `pnpm format:check` clean.

**Evidence after fix (Vitest output for the touched test files)**:

```
Tests  25 passed (25)
```

The 5 new tests cover: adapter 403 fallback returns text-link result and receipt; adapter non-403 re-throws; monitor 403 fallback with caption joins caption and URL; monitor 403 fallback URL-only sends bare URL; monitor non-403 still logs error and skips fallback.

**Observed result after fix**: `sendGoogleChatMessage` receives `"caption\nhttps://…"` (caption present) or `"https://…"` (caption absent) with no `attachments` field. `statusSink` is called once on successful fallback delivery. `runtime.error` is not called on the 403 path.

**What was not tested**: live Google Chat end-to-end with a real service-account credential and a space configured for app auth. The API contract — 403 for `media.upload` under `chat.bot` scope — is the source-level proof basis.

**Repro confirmation**: the 403 fallback tests fail on the pre-patch tree (`sendGoogleChatMessage` is not called; `runtime.error` is called instead) and pass with the fix applied, confirming the upload catch scope and `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` match are both required.

### Risk / Mitigation

- **Risk**: `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` fires on non-upload 403s. **Mitigation**: matches the literal prefix `"Google Chat upload 403:"` (set by `errorPrefix: "Google Chat upload"` at `api.ts:228`), not the generic `" 403:"` substring; `sendGoogleChatMessage` uses the default `"Google Chat API"` prefix so its 403s never match.
- **Risk**: Text-link fallback fires for non-scope upload failures. **Mitigation**: fallback is gated on `isUploadAuthScopeFailure` (403 only) AND a remote http(s) URL check; size, network, and other HTTP status failures still surface as errors.

### Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix

### Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] Google Chat plugin

### Linked Issue/PR

Fixes #89430

target.title: refactor: route bundled session helpers through accessor
target.body:
## What

Routes bundled plugin session consumers and SDK/runtime session helper APIs through the narrow session accessor seam while preserving the deprecated file-backed whole-store compatibility helpers.

## Why

This is part of Path 3 SQLite branch-by-abstraction work. The bundled plugin consumers needed narrow session entry access so SQLite validation can exercise the accessor seam without introducing a mutable SQLite whole-store JSON API.

Refs #88838.

## Changes

- Route SDK session helpers
- Route plugin runtime helpers
- Migrate bundled session callers
- Add lifecycle cleanup seam
- Preserve file-backed compatibility
- Cover accessor regressions

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.ts` | Routes narrow SDK helpers through accessor |
| `src/plugin-sdk/config-runtime.ts` | Routes config session reads through accessor |
| `src/plugins/runtime/runtime-agent.ts` | Exposes narrow runtime session helpers |
| `src/plugins/runtime/types-core.ts` | Adds runtime helper types |
| `src/config/sessions/session-accessor.ts` | Adds lifecycle cleanup exports |
| `src/config/sessions/store.ts` | Adds cleanup implementation support |
| `extensions/discord/src/monitor/*` | Migrates model/thread session paths |
| `extensions/telegram/src/*` | Migrates model/session metadata reads |
| `extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/*` | Migrates group activation entry paths |
| `extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.ts` | Uses lifecycle cleanup seam |
| `extensions/{feishu,matrix,mattermost,slack,codex}` | Uses narrowed session projections |
| `src/plugin-sdk/*test.ts`, `src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts` | Adds regression coverage |

## Testing

- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/plugin-sdk/config-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/session-store-runtime.test.ts src/plugins/runtime/index.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/native-command.model-picker.test.ts extensions/discord/src/monitor/thread-session-close.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot.create-telegram-bot.test.ts extensions/telegram/src/bot-native-commands.session-meta.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/auto-reply/monitor/group-activation.test.ts extensions/memory-core/src/dreaming-narrative.test.ts extensions/active-memory/index.test.ts`
- Expected: 8 focused file-backed shards pass.
- Disposable SQLite replay proof is green on `integration/sqlite-flip-bundled-plugin-consumers-replay` after `.18.1`, `.25`, `.26`, and `.10` validation; `.10` is closed in Pebbles.
- `git diff --check`
- Targeted formatter/lint/type/autoreview proof was recorded in the Path 3 progress log for this slice.
