target.title: feat(subagents): forward toolsAllow from sessions_spawn
target.body:
## Summary
- Add optional `toolsAllow` to `sessions_spawn` for native embedded subagent runs.
- Validate it as a string array, preserve explicit `[]`, and reject unsupported ACP or CLI-backed native paths instead of silently ignoring the policy.
- Forward and persist the allowlist through Gateway/session/embedded-attempt surfaces so later native child turns inherit the stored tool policy.
- Update protocol/docs/tests/prompt snapshots for the additive field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has broad May subagent/runtime work and existing embedded-runner `toolsAllow` support, but `sessions_spawn` still does not expose, forward, or persist a per-spawn `toolsAllow` entry point. This PR is narrowed to that missing public/control-plane plumbing.

## Change Type (select all)
- [x] Feature

## Scope (select all touched areas)
- [x] Gateway / orchestration
- [x] Skills / tool execution
- [x] API / contracts
- [x] Documentation

## Linked Issue/PR
- Related #78381
- [ ] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Real behavior proof (required for external PRs)

Behavior addressed: `sessions_spawn` callers can constrain native embedded subagent tools with `toolsAllow`; the same policy is persisted on child sessions and inherited by later embedded child turns when no explicit override is passed.

Real environment tested: macOS 26.2, Node v25.9.0, pnpm 11.2.2, local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `bf3ad1a9861ebe0ee4e0a90533d065cb69ae7201`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `openclaw.json`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local`, and repo mock OpenAI `/v1/responses` server with request logging.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Seeded a temp `sessions.json` child session at `agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139` with `runtimeToolsAllow: ["read", "exec"]`, started `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs` with `MOCK_REQUEST_LOG`, then ran `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH=/tmp/openclaw-78441-proof-current.itWvhi/state/openclaw.json OPENAI_API_KEY=sk-openclaw-78441-proof node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --session-key agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-470c152139 --message "Reply with exactly OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441" --json`, followed by a Node assertion over the mock request log.

Evidence after fix: Copied live output from the request-log assertion:

```json
{
  "head": "bf3ad1a9861ebe0ee4e0a90533d065cb69ae7201",
  "sessionKey": "agent:main:subagent:tools-policy-proof-bf3ad1a986",
  "responsesCalls": 1,
  "inheritedToolNames": [
    "exec",
    "read"
  ],
  "markerObserved": true
}
```



Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts --reporter=dot
2 Vitest shards passed: 215 tests passed
```

Observed result after fix: The later CLI turn succeeded, reached the mock `/v1/responses` endpoint exactly once, returned `OPENCLAW_E2E_OK_78441`, and the provider request exposed only the inherited `exec` and `read` tools from the persisted child-session `runtimeToolsAllow` field.

What was not tested: This proof used a mock OpenAI server instead of live OpenAI credentials and did not start a long-running Gateway process. Focused tests cover the `sessions_spawn` writer/forwarding path; this proof verifies the real later-turn CLI/provider request behavior.

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)
- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
  - [x] Seam / integration test
  - [x] Prompt snapshot check
- Target test or file: `src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts`, `src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts`, `src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts`, `src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts`, `src/commands/agent.test.ts`, `src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts`, `test/fixtures/agents/prompt-snapshots/codex-runtime-happy-path/*`
- Scenario the tests lock in: `toolsAllow` is exposed, normalized, validates string arrays, preserves explicit `[]`, rejects unsupported runtimes, forwards through Gateway/session state, reaches embedded runs started by the command runner, and appears in model-facing dynamic tool snapshots.

## User-visible / Behavior Changes
`runtime="subagent"` callers may pass `toolsAllow` to narrow a spawned native embedded subagent's tool set. Defaults are unchanged when omitted. `toolsAllow: []` intentionally runs the child without tools. Unsupported ACP and CLI-backed native paths reject the option clearly.

## Security Impact (required)
- New permissions/capabilities? `No`
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? `No`
- New/changed network calls? `No`
- Command/tool execution surface changed? `Yes`
- Data access scope changed? `No`
- If any `Yes`, explain risk + mitigation: this only narrows the child tool set for native embedded subagent runs. Unsupported paths reject the option so callers do not get a false sense that an allowlist was enforced.

## Repro + Verification
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/tools/sessions-spawn-tool.test.ts src/agents/openclaw-tools.subagents.sessions-spawn.lifecycle.test.ts src/agents/subagent-spawn.model-session.test.ts src/agents/command/attempt-execution.cli.test.ts src/commands/agent.test.ts src/gateway/server-methods/agent.test.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts --reporter=dot` passed: 5 files, 159 tests, 3 Vitest shards.
- `node scripts/run-tsgo.mjs -p test/tsconfig/tsconfig.core.test.json --incremental --tsBuildInfoFile .artifacts/tsgo-cache/core-test.tsbuildinfo` passed.
- `./node_modules/.bin/oxfmt --check ...changed files...` passed.
- `pnpm prompt:snapshots:check` passed: `Prompt snapshots are current (7 files)`.
- `node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-core.config.ts --reporter=dot src/agents/code-mode.test.ts` passed locally after CI showed a timeout/interrupt flake in that unrelated file: 1 file, 48 tests.
- `git diff --check origin/main...HEAD && git diff --check` passed.
- `pnpm build` passed before the snapshot commit; `pnpm openclaw agent --help` rebuilt dist successfully after the snapshot commit because the CLI detected a new git head.
- Current-head CLI/mock OpenAI proof above passed.

## Human Verification (required)
- Verified scenarios: schema exposure, native forwarding path, Gateway forwarding path, session persistence, embedded command runner propagation, persisted child-session allowlist inheritance, strict validation tests, ACP rejection tests, CLI-backed native fail-closed behavior for non-empty and empty allowlists, embedded empty-allowlist behavior, prompt snapshot update, formatter/typecheck/build/whitespace checks.
- Edge cases checked: omitted `toolsAllow`, explicit empty `[]`, blank strings trimmed/filtered, non-string entries rejected, non-array input rejected, ACP runtime rejected, CLI-backed native runtime rejected.
- What you did **not** verify: a live provider-backed spawned child run with real OpenAI credentials.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`

## Risks and Mitigations
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect ACP runtime spawns.
  - Mitigation: the tool rejects `toolsAllow` for `runtime="acp"` with a clear input error.
- Risk: callers may expect `toolsAllow` to affect CLI-backed native runs.
  - Mitigation: CLI-backed native runs reject `toolsAllow` before dispatch because they cannot enforce the embedded-runner allowlist.
- Risk: public API naming/semantics need maintainer judgment.
  - Mitigation: the implementation is additive and reuses the existing embedded-runner allowlist semantics rather than adding a second filtering mechanism.

target.title: perf(agents): cache provider tool schema normalization
target.body:
## Summary
- Cache provider-normalized tool schema `parameters` for repeated embedded agent turns when the active provider hook exposes a stable hook-owned cache key.
- Keep the cache conservative: the key includes provider hook identity plus provider/model/runtime inputs and ordered original tool schema signatures; cached values clone only tool `parameters`, not tool objects, execute closures, approval state, or guards.
- Add `OPENCLAW_TOOL_SCHEMA_CACHE=0` as a kill switch, SDK docs/API surface for `resolveToolSchemaCacheKey`, and focused cache-key, clone-safety, env-disable, eviction, and runtime-handle coverage.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: repeated embedded agent turns no longer redo provider tool-schema normalization when the provider hook identity, provider/model/config inputs, and ordered tool schema signature are unchanged. Fresh tool objects, execute closures, approval state, and runtime guards are still rebuilt per turn.

Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout on macOS, branch `tool-schema-cache-pr3`, current head `ba4177c66ac8a4e7ad6ecd55c44282202bb64fe8`, with a local mock OpenAI Responses server and the real `runEmbeddedAgent` OpenAI Responses path.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Ran one real embedded-agent turn against the local mock OpenAI Responses server with `tools.allow: ["exec", "read"]`; the mock model requested the real OpenClaw `exec` tool with `command: "printf OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_TOOL_OK"`, received the tool output through `function_call_output`, then returned `OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_PROOF_OK`. In the same script, reset the provider tool schema cache and called the real `normalizeProviderToolSchemas` twice with matching schema signatures and different execute closures. Also reran the local file-safety CI shard after fixing the watch-node fake-child test hang.

Evidence after fix:

```json
{
  "head": "ba4177c66ac8a4e7ad6ecd55c44282202bb64fe8",
  "realRunVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_PROOF_OK",
  "realRunResponseCalls": 2,
  "realRunModelFacingToolNames": ["exec", "read"],
  "realRunToolOutputs": ["OPENCLAW_SCHEMA_CACHE_TOOL_OK"],
  "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
  "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true,
  "cacheStatsAfterFirstNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 0, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "cacheStatsAfterSecondNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 1, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true
}
```


Current-head runtime cache assertion after refreshing the generated Plugin SDK API baseline hash:

```json
{
  "head": "ba4177c66ac8a4e7ad6ecd55c44282202bb64fe8",
  "normalizeCalls": 1,
  "cacheStatsAfterFirstNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 0, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "cacheStatsAfterSecondNormalize": {"bypass": 0, "hit": 1, "miss": 1, "store": 1, "size": 1},
  "normalizedParametersEqual": true,
  "secondRunExecuteClosurePreserved": true,
  "cacheHitIncreasedOnSecondNormalize": true
}
```

Current-head verification commands after this patch:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/tool-schema-runtime.test.ts src/plugin-sdk/provider-tools.test.ts src/plugins/provider-runtime.test.ts --reporter=dot
3 Vitest shards passed: 83 tests passed
```

```text
node scripts/run-vitest.mjs src/infra/watch-node.test.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 1 file / 15 tests

OPENCLAW_VITEST_INCLUDE_FILE=/tmp/openclaw-file-safety-include.json OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=2 OPENCLAW_VITEST_SHARD_NAME=core-runtime-infra-file-safety OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=120000 OPENCLAW_TEST_PROJECTS_PARALLEL=2 pnpm exec node scripts/test-projects.mjs test/vitest/vitest.infra.config.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 4 files / 43 tests

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs ui/src/ui/app-gateway.node.test.ts
1 Vitest shard passed: 1 file / 61 tests

git diff --check
exit 0
```

Observed result after fix: the real embedded OpenAI Responses run exposed `exec` and `read` to the model, executed `exec` successfully, returned the tool output to the model as `function_call_output`, and completed the final assistant response. The direct runtime cache assertion then proved the first matching normalization was a miss/store and the second was a hit while preserving the fresh execute closure from the second tool object.

What was not tested: full live-provider latency was not tested. This PR caches only provider-normalized schema `parameters`; it does not cache complete tool inventory/materialization, policy resolution, prompt inputs, or full agent-turn startup.

commands.author: guanbear

target.title: feat(embedded-runner): expose prep stage timings
target.body:
## Summary
- Expose embedded runner prep stage snapshots on `EmbeddedRunAttemptResult`.
- Include the same prep stage summary on the `agent_end` hook event for plugin observability.
- Add focused coverage for attempt result and hook event prep stage propagation.
- Document the new optional hook field. No `CHANGELOG.md` change is kept.

## Current upstream status
Latest `origin/main` already has May embedded-runner/subagent performance work and trace logging for startup stages. It still does not expose the attempt prep stage summary to `agent_end` hook observers. This PR is narrowed to that missing diagnostics surface so plugins can collect stage timing evidence without patching core locally.

## Motivation
OpenClaw records detailed embedded runner prep stages internally, but that timing data is not available to plugin hooks. When embedded runs feel slow, plugin authors and operators cannot tell whether time is going into tool bundling, system prompt construction, session resource loading, or stream setup without adding local instrumentation.

Exposing the existing stage snapshot gives downstream integrations a low-risk way to collect latency evidence before proposing behavior-changing optimizations.

## Implementation notes
- Reuses the existing `createEmbeddedRunStageTracker()` / `prepStages.snapshot()` path.
- Keeps all new fields optional.
- Uses public structural plugin hook types instead of exporting runner internals through the plugin API.
- Does not change tool bundling, system prompt construction, stream setup, model calls, or runner behavior.
- Documents `agent_end.prepStages` as optional diagnostic telemetry whose stage labels may grow over time.

## Real behavior proof

Behavior addressed: Embedded runner prep stage timings are visible to a real non-bundled plugin's `agent_end` hook without changing the embedded run outcome.

Real environment tested: macOS local OpenClaw source checkout, current PR head `0e727513dca79812aab348f298cc4f9caa1a4447`, temporary `OPENCLAW_STATE_DIR`, temporary `OPENCLAW_CONFIG_PATH`, temporary local plugin loaded through `plugins.load.paths` with `hooks.allowConversationAccess=true`, typed lifecycle hook registration via `api.on("agent_end", ...)`, `node scripts/run-node.mjs agent --local --json`, and the repo mock OpenAI-compatible server `scripts/e2e/mock-openai-server.mjs`.

Exact steps or command run after this patch: Started the mock OpenAI server with `SUCCESS_MARKER=OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, ran a local embedded agent turn against a temporary config, and had the temporary plugin write the real `agent_end` event to JSONL. The proof script asserted both the CLI result and plugin hook payload.

Fresh current-head verification:

```text
node --max-old-space-size=8192 --import tsx scripts/generate-plugin-sdk-api-baseline.ts --check
OK docs/.generated/plugin-sdk-api-baseline.sha256

node scripts/run-vitest.mjs run --config test/vitest/vitest.agents-embedded-agent.config.ts src/agents/embedded-agent-runner/run/attempt.spawn-workspace.context-engine.test.ts -t "exposes embedded prep stages" --reporter=dot
1 passed, 56 skipped

pnpm exec oxfmt --check --threads=1 $(git diff --name-only upstream/main...HEAD)
All matched files use the correct format.

git diff --check upstream/main...HEAD
exit 0
```

Evidence after fix: copied live terminal output from the current-head local OpenClaw CLI run and typed non-bundled plugin `agent_end` hook JSONL assertion.

```json
{
  "head": "0e727513dca79812aab348f298cc4f9caa1a4447",
  "finalAssistantVisibleText": "OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK",
  "hookSuccess": true,
  "hookHasPrepStages": true,
  "hookTotalMs": 142,
  "hookStageNames": [
    "workspace-sandbox",
    "skills",
    "core-plugin-tools",
    "bootstrap-context",
    "bundle-tools",
    "system-prompt",
    "session-resource-loader",
    "agent-session",
    "stream-setup"
  ],
  "proofDir": "/tmp/openclaw-78381-proof-current.ppmsjm"
}
```

Observed result after fix: A standalone embedded `openclaw agent --local` run completed successfully against the mock OpenAI server, returned `OPENCLAW_PREP_PROOF_OK`, and the temporary plugin's real typed `agent_end` handler observed `event.prepStages` with total setup time and expected prep stages.

What was not tested: A live paid external model/provider run was not run. The proof used OpenClaw's deterministic local OpenAI-compatible response server to avoid provider latency/cost while still exercising the real embedded runner, local CLI, plugin loader, and typed `agent_end` hook dispatch.

## Compatibility / Migration
- Backward compatible? `Yes`
- Config/env changes? `No`
- Migration needed? `No`
- Risk: plugin authors might treat stage names as permanent.
- Mitigation: docs describe `prepStages` as optional diagnostics and allow stage labels to grow over time.

commands.author: Jerry-Xin

target.title: fix: skip setup-only channel plugins in outbound resolution and drain pending deliveries on qqbot reconnect
target.body:
## Summary

When the qqbot WebSocket reconnects after a session timeout (close code `4009`), `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()` returns a setup-only plugin stub that lacks any send capability. This causes the `"Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"` error, and all messages queued during the disconnection window are permanently lost.

## Root Cause

`resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()` resolves the first matching plugin entry for a channel id without verifying that the entry actually has send capability (`outbound.sendText`, `message.send.text`, etc.). During qqbot WebSocket reconnection, a setup-only plugin entry (registered for configuration/pairing but not for message delivery) shadows the real sending plugin, causing the resolution to short-circuit with a non-functional stub.

Regression introduced in 2026.5.28 (commit `e932160`).

## Fix

### 1. Split outbound channel resolution into shared and delivery paths (`channel-resolution.ts`)

Add a `channelPluginCanSend()` predicate that checks for any of the 10 known send methods across both the legacy `outbound` adapter and the newer `message.send` adapter:

- `outbound.sendText / sendMedia / sendPayload / sendPoll / sendFormattedText / sendFormattedMedia`
- `message.send.text / media / payload / poll`

Instead of gating the shared `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()` resolver (which would break action-only channel plugins that have `actions.handleAction` but no send capability), a new **`resolveOutboundChannelPluginForDelivery()`** resolver applies the send-capability guard exclusively for message delivery paths.

- **`resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()`**: Shared resolver — returns any matching plugin regardless of send capability, preserving resolution for action dispatch, target resolution, and other non-send callers.
- **`resolveOutboundChannelPluginForDelivery()`**: Delivery resolver — skips setup-only entries and only returns plugins that can actually send messages.
- **`resolveOutboundChannelMessageAdapter()`**: Updated to use the delivery resolver.
- **`message.ts`**: `sendMessage`/`sendPoll` updated to use the delivery resolver.

### 2. Drain pending deliveries on qqbot reconnect (`extensions/qqbot/src/channel.ts`)

The `onReady` and `onResumed` gateway callbacks now call `drainPendingDeliveries()` from the plugin SDK delivery queue runtime, recovering messages that were queued while the WebSocket was disconnected. The drain is:

- Filtered by channel (`qqbot`) and account id, with a fallback for default-account entries where `accountId` may be `undefined`
- Deduplicated via `drainKey` to prevent concurrent drains
- Wrapped in try/catch so drain failures do not break the reconnection flow

## Tests

- `channel-resolution.test.ts`: 17 tests (5 new — setup-only plugin returns from shared resolver, action-only plugin resolves from shared but not delivery resolver, delivery resolver skips setup-only and returns sending fallback, legacy outbound adapter, poll-only outbound)
- `channel.test.ts`: 2 tests (new file — onReady drain, onResumed drain)

Fixes #88955.


## Real behavior proof

- **Behavior or issue addressed**: After a QQ Bot WebSocket disconnect (session takeover / close code 4009), `resolveOutboundChannelPlugin()` returned a setup-only plugin stub instead of the runtime entry, causing `"Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"` and dropping all pending deliveries.

- **Real environment tested**: Local patched OpenClaw `2026.6.2` (commit `b90683e`, branch `fix/qqbot-reconnect-outbound-88955`), macOS arm64, Node.js v24.10.0. QQ Bot plugin `@openclaw/qqbot@2026.5.31-beta.4` with live QQ Bot credentials (appId `<redacted>`).

- **Exact steps or command run after this patch**:
  1. Built patched OpenClaw from fix branch: `pnpm build && npm pack --ignore-scripts`
  2. Installed globally: `npm install -g openclaw-2026.6.2.tgz` — confirmed version: `OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (b90683e)`
  3. Restarted gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  4. Observed qqbot plugin startup, WebSocket connection, and `Gateway ready` callback sequence in logs
  5. Verified normal QQ Bot C2C message round-trip: user sent "hello" → bot received → processed → replied with markdown (20 chars, 200 OK)
  6. Scanned full gateway log for `"Outbound not configured"` errors — zero matches
  7. Repeated restart cycle to confirm reconnect stability across multiple `Gateway ready` events

- **Evidence after fix**:
  ```
  # Patched OpenClaw installed and running
  $ openclaw --version
  OpenClaw 2026.6.2 (b90683e)

  # Gateway restart sequence — qqbot reconnects cleanly
  2026-06-02T22:38:53.727+08:00 [gateway] received SIGTERM; restarting
  2026-06-02T22:39:15.986+08:00 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: ..., qqbot, ...; 1.6s)
  2026-06-02T22:39:16.171+08:00 [qqbot] [qqbot:default] Starting gateway — appId=<redacted>, enabled=true
  2026-06-02T22:39:17.387+08:00 [qqbot] [default] Connecting to wss://api.sgroup.qq.com/websocket
  2026-06-02T22:39:18.468+08:00 [qqbot] [default] WebSocket connected
  2026-06-02T22:39:18.641+08:00 [qqbot] [qqbot:default] Gateway ready

  # Successful QQ Bot message delivery after reconnect
  2026-06-02T22:32:40.229+08:00 [qqbot] [default] Processing message from <redacted>: hello
  2026-06-02T22:32:59.431+08:00 [qqbot] [default] [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK
  2026-06-02T22:32:59.432+08:00 [qqbot] [default] Sent markdown chunk (20/20 chars) with 0 HTTP images (c2c)

  # Zero outbound resolution errors across entire log history
  $ grep -c "Outbound not configured.*qqbot" ~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log
  0

  # Multiple Gateway ready events over 4+ hours — all clean reconnects
  $ grep "qqbot.*Gateway ready" ~/Library/Logs/openclaw/gateway.log | wc -l
  18
  ```

- **Observed result after fix**: The delivery resolver correctly skipped the setup-only plugin entry (which has no `sendText`/`sendMedia` methods) and resolved the runtime entry with full send capability. Outbound messages delivered successfully through the QQ Bot API (HTTP 200). Zero occurrences of the `"Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"` error across 18 reconnect cycles over a 4+ hour observation window. The shared resolver continued to return all plugin entries for non-send callers (action dispatch, target resolution), preserving action-only plugin compatibility.

- **What was not tested**:

  Live drain proof is not reproducible on the fixed build. The `drainReconnectPendingDeliveries()` mechanism (Fix 2) was verified via unit tests but not observed draining actual queued entries in a live environment. This is not an oversight — it is a structural consequence of the resolver fix (Fix 1) eliminating the only deterministic cause of pending qqbot entries. Code-level analysis confirms this:

  **How the delivery queue works** (`deliver.ts:1254-1280`): Every outbound delivery is write-ahead enqueued via `enqueueDelivery()` before any send attempt. On success, `ackDelivery()` removes the entry (`deliver.ts:1367`). On throw, `failDelivery()` bumps `retryCount` and **leaves it pending** (`delivery-queue-storage.ts:137-144`).

  **What created the pending entries in #88955**: The `"Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"` throw fired from `createChannelHandler` (`deliver.ts:223-231`) when `resolveOutboundChannelPluginForDelivery` returned a setup-only plugin with no send methods. This throw occurs **outside** the per-payload try/catch (`deliver.ts:1451`), so it propagated to the queue-cleanup catch → `failDelivery` → entry left pending. The resolver fix eliminates this throw by filtering setup-only entries via `channelPluginCanSend()`.

  **Why transient QQ API failures also do not create pending entries**: The qqbot engine **swallows** HTTP/network errors into returned `{error}` result objects instead of throwing (`outbound.ts:301-304, 313-318`). The delivery core text branch records "sent" purely on `deliveredResults.length > 0` with no message-id identity gate (`deliver.ts:1772-1778`), so a swallowed engine error yields a "sent" outcome → `ackDelivery` → entry removed, not pending.

  **The drain's real value**: It is a belt-and-suspenders safeguard for the **upgrade path** — entries left pending (with `lastError: "Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"`) by an unfixed build are drained immediately after a fixed gateway reconnects. The `bypassBackoff` predicate explicitly matches this exact error string (`/outbound not configured/i`), ensuring these entries skip retry backoff and are retried on the first reconnect event. If the retry still fails (e.g., the plugin is genuinely misconfigured), `isPermanentDeliveryError` (`delivery-queue-recovery.ts:79`) moves the entry to `failed/` — no infinite retry loop. `MAX_RETRIES` is also enforced before the bypass branch (`delivery-queue-recovery.ts:544`). The drain also covers the narrow crash-in-enqueue-to-ack window handled by `recoverPendingDeliveries` at startup (`delivery-queue-recovery.ts:604`). The drain selection filter (`channel === "qqbot"` + account id match + bypassBackoff for `"Outbound not configured"`, `channel.test.ts:90-152`) and the reconnect-drain integration logic (`delivery-queue.reconnect-drain.test.ts`) are exercised by 35 unit tests (all passing).

  **Seeded pending-drain proof**: To validate the drain recovery path end-to-end, a pending delivery entry was manually seeded into the SQLite delivery queue to simulate the upgrade scenario (entries left by an unfixed build):

  1. Inserted a pending entry: `id=seeded-drain-test-001`, `channel=qqbot`, `accountId=default`, `lastError="Outbound not configured for channel: qqbot"`, `retry_count=1`
  2. Restarted gateway: `openclaw gateway restart`
  3. Observed recovery sequence in logs:

  ```
  00:30:00.353 [gateway] http server listening (8 plugins: ..., qqbot, ...; 1.9s)
  00:30:00.987 [delivery-recovery] Found 1 pending delivery entries — starting recovery
  00:30:01.567 [qqbot] [qqbot:api] <<< Status: 200 OK
  00:30:02.703 [delivery-recovery] Recovered delivery seeded-drain-test-001 on qqbot
  00:30:02.703 [delivery-recovery] Delivery recovery complete: 1 recovered, 0 failed, 0 skipped (max retries), 0 deferred (backoff)
  00:30:02.816 [qqbot] [default] WebSocket connected
  00:30:02.952 [qqbot] [qqbot:default] Gateway ready
  ```

  4. Verified entry removed from DB: `SELECT count(*) FROM delivery_queue_entries WHERE channel='qqbot' AND status='pending'` → **0**
  5. Zero `"Outbound not configured"` errors after restart

  **Key observation**: The `0 deferred (backoff)` in the recovery summary confirms the `bypassBackoff` predicate (`/outbound not configured/i`) correctly matched the seeded entry's `lastError`, allowing immediate retry without waiting for backoff. The entry was successfully delivered (HTTP 200) and acked (removed from queue).

  **Note**: The startup `recoverPendingDeliveries` path (`delivery-queue-recovery.ts:604`) executed before the qqbot `onReady` callback (`00:30:00.987` vs `00:30:02.952`), so startup recovery handled the entry before the reconnect drain fired. Both paths share the same underlying drain logic and bypass predicates; the startup path simply runs first. A naturally-occurring live drain on the `onReady` path cannot be reliably reproduced because the fix removes the deterministic failure that created those entries, and startup recovery pre-empts the reconnect drain for entries present at boot. Multi-shard configurations were not tested.

target.title: fix: split image setup and request timeout semantics
target.body:
## Summary
- split image-description timeout handling so setup and provider request both use the configured `tools.media.image.timeoutSeconds` budget instead of shrinking the request budget by elapsed setup time
- preserve the full configured timeout for the provider request instead of silently subtracting setup latency down to a tiny remaining budget
- add focused tests covering request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and slow-setup request behavior
- keep public docs user-facing: the image timeout still applies to the request itself and is not reduced by earlier preparation work
- keep the heavier image-tool latency and runtime-loading reductions for follow-up PRs after this timeout-semantics fix

## Verification
- `git diff --check`
- `env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 node scripts/crabbox-wrapper.mjs run --provider docker --docker-image node:22-bookworm --timing-json --shell -- "bash -lc 'corepack enable >/dev/null 2>&1 && env CI=1 NODE_OPTIONS=--max-old-space-size=4096 OPENCLAW_VITEST_MAX_WORKERS=1 OPENCLAW_VITEST_NO_OUTPUT_TIMEOUT_MS=900000 corepack pnpm test src/media-understanding/image.test.ts'"`

## Real behavior proof
Behavior addressed: image timeout errors no longer collapse setup latency into a tiny remaining provider timeout; setup and request failures now report distinct phases.
Real environment tested: Crabbox `docker` provider with `node:22-bookworm`, synced from this worktree.
Exact steps or command run after this patch: run the targeted media-understanding Vitest file via Crabbox Docker using the command above.
Evidence after fix: `src/media-understanding/image.test.ts` passes with 25 tests; assertions cover request timeout messaging, setup timeout messaging, and a slow setup that still passes the full configured timeout through to the provider request.
Observed result after fix: the test suite passed, provider-request calls still received `timeoutMs: 1000` after a mocked `400ms` setup delay, and timeout messages now distinguish `setup` vs `request`.
What was not tested: live provider behavior and the follow-up PRs that will reduce the image runtime-loading latency itself.

target.title: fix(cli): avoid false local gateway unreachable on probe timeout
target.body:
## Summary
- separate local gateway reachability from follow-up detail/read probe failures
- add a bounded direct status-RPC fallback or degraded classification for local status/probe paths when the default probe times out or returns unknown capability but the gateway is otherwise RPC-capable
- add regression coverage for the #73535 split: default local probe false-negative while `--require-rpc` or direct `status` RPC succeeds

## Scope
This intentionally excludes unauthenticated loopback auth relaxation from #64400, which is routed to central security triage. The fix should stay in the ordinary status/probe classification and fallback path.

## Credit
Fixes the non-security path tracked in #48360. Uses reproduction evidence from #62762 and #73535, with historical evidence from #51357 and #42019. Thanks @RacecarGuy, @justinschille, @DJBlackhawk, @tianyaqpzm, and @0xrsydn.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(commitments): stop hardcoding fastMode in extraction (#78451)
target.body:
## Summary

MiniMax Coding Plan operators whose plan owns `MiniMax-M2.7` but not the `-highspeed` variant hit `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` on every commitment extraction pass, because the commitments extraction path passed `fastMode: true` unconditionally to `runEmbeddedPiAgent`, which caused the MiniMax stream wrapper's `createMinimaxFastModeWrapper` to rewrite the base model id to `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`.

## Root Cause

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts:242` (pre-fix): the call to `runEmbeddedPiAgent` for hidden commitment extraction hardcoded `fastMode: true`.
- `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24`: the MiniMax stream wrapper accepts a `fastMode` boolean and, when true, swaps the base model id to its `-highspeed` variant.
- Result: every commitments extraction request silently flipped `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, which Coding Plan operators do not own. MiniMax responds with HTTP 500 / error code 2061. Reporter verified the Coding Plan owns the base model but not highspeed.
- The reporter listed three valid fix shapes (drop the hardcode, add a config field, or inherit agent fastMode). This PR uses option 1: dropping the hardcode lets the existing `resolveFastModeState` helper continue to control fast-mode for the rest of the agent runtime, and commitments extraction inherits the same default-false behavior.

## Changes

- `src/commitments/runtime.ts`: remove `fastMode: true` from the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload built in the commitments extraction path. Add a comment explaining the reason so this does not regress.
- `src/commitments/runtime.test.ts`: extend the existing `runEmbeddedPiAgentMock` assertion to assert `fastMode` is **not** in the payload, locking in the regression fix.
- `CHANGELOG.md`: single-line entry under Unreleased / Fixes.

## Real behavior proof

- Behavior addressed: On every commitment extraction pass, MiniMax Coding Plan operators receive `HTTP 500: your current token plan not support model, MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (2061)` from the MiniMax server because the agent runtime requested `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed` even though their plan only owns `MiniMax-M2.7`. The reporter (`@advaitpaliwal`) verified `commitments.enabled: true, commitments.maxPerDay: 10` config, then ran a real Coding Plan session, observed extraction always selecting the highspeed variant, and traced the root cause to `dist/runtime-CXt7BFiH.js` line 386 (`fastMode: true` hardcoded). Issue: https://github.com/openclaw/openclaw/issues/78451
- Real environment tested: Local OpenClaw checkout at `../openclaw-78451` on Windows 11 + Node 22.14. The smoke script reads the **patched production file `src/commitments/runtime.ts` directly from the worktree**, locates the entire `defaultExtractBatch` function (the path that builds the `runEmbeddedPiAgent` payload), prints its SHA-256 + verbatim bytes, then runs structural assertions over the captured payload literal to prove `fastMode: true` is gone, the `#78451` explanatory comment is in place, and every sibling field is preserved. No mocks, no test runner — the patched source bytes drive the smoke. PR head: `a3189f329687fa8c2c9da783afbc0d644ea94573`.
- Exact steps or command run after this patch: `git checkout fix/commitments-no-fastmode-hardcode && node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts`, where `smoke-pr78459.mts` reads `src/commitments/runtime.ts`, slices the `async function defaultExtractBatch` body, hashes it, prints the verbatim payload object literal, and runs structural assertions against the live patched source.
- Evidence after fix: Terminal output captured locally on PR head `a3189f3296` (Windows 11 + Node 22.14, the patched `src/commitments/runtime.ts` `defaultExtractBatch` function read from the worktree, 1660 bytes, SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379`).

~~~text
$ node --experimental-strip-types ./smoke-pr78459.mts
=== Patched defaultExtractBatch function — bytes from src/commitments/runtime.ts ===
offset: 7245 -> 8905 ( 1660 bytes)
SHA-256: ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379

function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false
explanatory '#78451' comment present                : true
  preserved field 'disableTools: true': true
  preserved field 'thinkLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'verboseLevel: "off"': true
  preserved field 'reasoningLevel: "off"': true

=== Payload object passed to runEmbeddedPiAgent (verbatim from patched code) ===
{ cfg, agentId: first.agentId });
  const result = await runEmbeddedPiAgent({
    sessionId: runId,
    sessionKey: `agent:${first.agentId}:commitments:${runId}`,
    agentId: first.agentId,
    trigger: "manual",
    sessionFile: resolveExtractionSessionFile(first.agentId, runId),
    workspaceDir: resolveAgentWorkspaceDir(cfg, first.agentId),
    config: cfg,
    provider: modelRef.provider,
    model: modelRef.model,
    prompt: buildCommitmentExtractionPrompt({ cfg, items: params.items }),
    disableTools: true,
    thinkLevel: "off",
    verboseLevel: "off",
    reasoningLevel: "off",
    // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
    // commitments extraction inherit the configured fast-mode state (which
    // defaults to false). Hardcoding true caused MiniMax Coding Plans without
    // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
    // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
    timeoutMs: resolved.extraction.timeoutSeconds * 1000,
    runId,
    bootstrapContextMode: "lightweight",
    skillsSnapshot: { prompt: "", skills: [] },
    suppressToolErrorWarnings: true,
  }

=== fastMode-related lines in the payload (BEFORE-vs-AFTER comparison) ===
  // Do not hardcode fastMode here: leaving the field undefined lets the
  // highspeed access to fail with HTTP 500 because the MiniMax stream wrapper
  // would rewrite MiniMax-M2.7 -> MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed (#78451).
~~~

- Observed result after fix: The patched `defaultExtractBatch` function — pinned by a 1660-byte / SHA-256 `ae3690fc3189a7bc3e6d33e8693a3a28aa24ee3c7a82822618c5f1c762bcb379` integrity check — has zero `fastMode: true` literals (`function body still contains literal 'fastMode: true': false`). The explanatory `#78451` comment is in place (`explanatory '#78451' comment present: true`), and every sibling field (`disableTools: true`, `thinkLevel: "off"`, `verboseLevel: "off"`, `reasoningLevel: "off"`) is preserved verbatim. The captured payload literal shown verbatim above confirms `runEmbeddedPiAgent` is now called without a `fastMode` key — so the MiniMax stream wrapper at `src/agents/pi-embedded-runner/minimax-stream-wrappers.ts:24` no longer flips `MiniMax-M2.7` → `MiniMax-M2.7-highspeed`, and the operator's configured base model id flows through unchanged.
- What was not tested: A live HTTP roundtrip against the real MiniMax server with a Coding Plan token is not available on the contributor machine, so this is source-level / structural proof against the patched payload-builder, not a live MiniMax 200 response. Maintainers may apply `proof: override` if a live MiniMax recording is required; the reporter's original issue already contains live HTTP 500 evidence from the real Coding Plan.

## Test

- `pnpm test src/commitments/runtime.test.ts` — 6 passed (including the new `expect(callArgs).not.toHaveProperty("fastMode")` regression assertion).

## Notes

- Scope: smallest complete fix. No new config surface and no provider/wrapper edits — the existing fast-mode resolution chain already covers the right behavior; the hardcode was the only thing forcing `true`.
- Compatibility: agents that need fast-mode for commitments can still set it via `agents.defaults.models.<id>.params.fastMode: true` (or per-agent `fastModeDefault: true`) — the `resolveFastModeState` chain is unchanged.

Closes #78451

target.title: fix(barnacle): resync candidate labels on ready_for_review, remove stale labels
target.body:
## Summary

- Problem: Barnacle auto-response doesn't resync candidate labels when a draft PR is marked `ready_for_review`, leaving stale candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) on PRs with meaningful bodies.
- Why it matters: Draft PRs auto-labeled with `triage: blank-template` keep that label after the author fills in the template and clicks "Ready for review", confusing triage workflow.
- What changed: Added `ready_for_review` to the auto-response workflow trigger and script allowlist; rewrote `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` to compute and remove stale candidate labels while preserving suppression/privileged-author guards.
- What did NOT change: No changes to existing candidate label classification rules, maintainer/OWNER skip logic, or `bad-barnacle` suppression.

## Change Type (select all)

- [x] Bug fix
- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Scope (select all touched areas)

- [x] CI/CD / infra

## Linked Issue/PR

- Closes #77343
- [x] This PR fixes a bug or regression

## Root Cause (if applicable)

- Root cause: Three gaps — (1) `.github/workflows/auto-response.yml` did not include `ready_for_review` in `pull_request_target` types, (2) `isPrCandidateEvent` allowlist in `barnacle-auto-response.mjs` did not include `ready_for_review`, and (3) `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` was add-only (never removed stale labels).
- Missing detection / guardrail: No test for the `ready_for_review` → stale label removal path.
- Contributing context (if known): N/A

## Real behavior proof

Not applicable — this PR modifies only GitHub Actions workflow automation (`.github/workflows/auto-response.yml`).
No product code, CLI, gateway, plugin, or UI surface is touched.
Barnacle + changelog unit tests cover the changed logic (30/30 + 15/15 passed).

## Regression Test Plan (if applicable)

- Coverage level that should have caught this:
  - [x] Unit test
- Target test or file: `test/scripts/barnacle-auto-response.test.ts`
- Scenario the test should lock in: `ready_for_review` action removes stale candidate labels when PR body becomes meaningful
- Why this is the smallest reliable guardrail: Directly exercises `applyPullRequestCandidateLabels` return value + `removeLabels` call path
- Existing test that already covers this (if any): None — this was a new coverage gap
- If no new test is added, why not: N/A

## User-visible / Behavior Changes

When a maintainer-owned PR transitions from draft to `ready_for_review` (or is edited), Barnacle recomputes candidate labels and removes any stale Barnacle-owned candidate labels (e.g. `triage: blank-template`) that no longer match the PR body/files.

## Diagram (if applicable)

```text
Before:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [add missing labels only] -> [stale labels persist]

After:
[PR ready_for_review] -> [recompute labels] -> [add missing + remove stale] -> [labels match body]
```

## Security Impact (required)

- New permissions/capabilities? (`Yes/No`) No
- Secrets/tokens handling changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- New/changed network calls? (`Yes/No`) No (existing GitHub API calls)
- Command/tool execution surface changed? (`Yes/No`) No
- Data access scope changed? (`Yes/No`) No

## Repro + Verification

### Environment

- OS: any (GitHub Actions)
- Runtime/container: Node 24 (GitHub Actions)
- Model/provider: N/A
- Integration/channel: GitHub auto-response workflow

### Steps

1. Open a draft PR with a blank issue template body
2. Barnacle auto-labels it with `triage: blank-template`
3. Edit body to fill in the template (Problem/Why/What)
4. Click "Ready for review"

### Expected

- `triage: blank-template` is removed; correct candidate labels applied

### Actual

- `triage: blank-template` persisted (before fix)

## Evidence

- [x] Failing test/log before + passing after

## Human Verification (required)

- Verified scenarios: `ready_for_review` with meaningful body removes stale labels; `ready_for_review` recomputes candidate labels; `bad-barnacle` suppresses removal; maintainer-authored PRs not mutated
- Edge cases checked: `bad-barnacle` suppression, maintainer/OWNER/COLLABORATOR skip, mixed candidate label transitions
- What you did **not** verify: Live GitHub workflow run (requires merge to test end-to-end)

## Compatibility / Migration

- Backward compatible? (`Yes`)
- Config/env changes? (`No`)
- Migration needed? (`No`)

## Risks and Mitigations

- Risk: Stale label removal could incorrectly strip a label if `classifyPullRequestCandidateLabels` has a bug.
  - Mitigation: Removal only targets Barnacle-owned candidate labels (`candidateLabelValues`); `bad-barnacle` and privileged-author skips preserved; full test suite covers all paths.

target.title: [codex] Fix Anthropic Vertex npm audit regression
target.body:
## Summary

- remove the `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk` runtime dependency from the shipped OpenClaw package
- replace it with a small local Anthropic Vertex client built on the existing `@anthropic-ai/sdk` and `google-auth-library@10.6.2`
- update the lockfile so the npm package no longer pulls `google-auth-library@9 -> gaxios@6 -> uuid@9`

## Why

`openclaw@2026.5.2-beta.1` introduced a clean npm audit regression for consumers: `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk@0.16.0` depends on `google-auth-library@^9.4.2`, which resolves to `gaxios@6.7.1` and `uuid@9.0.1`. npm ignores dependency package `overrides`, so the published OpenClaw package still audited with two moderate findings even though the pnpm workspace override resolved `uuid` internally.

## Validation

- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`
- `pnpm exec vitest run extensions/anthropic-vertex`
- `pnpm exec oxlint extensions/anthropic-vertex/stream-runtime.ts`
- `pnpm audit --prod --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- packed this branch with `npm pack`, installed the tarball into a fresh npm project, and verified `npm audit --omit=dev --json` reports 0 vulnerabilities
- tarball install dependency tree no longer contains `@anthropic-ai/vertex-sdk`, `google-auth-library@9`, `gaxios@6`, or `uuid@9`

target.title: fix(feishu): use union_id as fallback for @mention detection in multi-bot groups
target.body:
## Problem

Fixes #40768

When multiple Feishu bots share the same group, @mentioning a specific bot is not recognized. The bot logs `message in group ... did not mention bot` even though it's correctly mentioned.

## Root Cause

Feishu `open_id` is **app-scoped** — the same bot has a different `open_id` depending on which app's token is used to query it. Confirmed by querying the same message with two different bot tokens:

```
# Same @mention, different app contexts:
writer token → mention open_id: ou_4ede388c79638a9057a432c3e3832f91
tron token   → mention open_id: ou_04dc13327d491d9e36da57826a4131f0
```

WebSocket push events deliver mention `open_id`s in a specific app context that may not match the receiving bot's own `open_id`, causing `checkBotMentioned()` to always return `false`.

## Fix

`union_id` is globally unique across all apps for the same entity — it doesn't vary by app context. This PR:

1. Fetches `union_id` alongside `open_id` from `bot/v3/info` at startup
2. Stores it in a new `botUnionIds` map in monitor state
3. Passes it through to `checkBotMentioned()` as a fallback
4. Matches mentions by `union_id` when `open_id` doesn't match

The `open_id` match is preserved as the primary check; `union_id` is a fallback, so existing behavior is unchanged for single-bot setups.

## Files Changed

- `types.ts`: add `botUnionId` to `FeishuProbeResult`
- `probe.ts`: extract `union_id` from `bot/v3/info` response
- `monitor.startup.ts`: propagate `botUnionId` through `FeishuMonitorBotIdentity`
- `monitor.state.ts`: add `botUnionIds` map with proper clear/delete
- `monitor.account.ts`: store/clear `botUnionId`, pass to `handleFeishuMessage`
- `bot.ts`: accept `botUnionId` in `checkBotMentioned` and `parseFeishuMessageEvent`, match by `union_id` when `open_id` mismatches
target.author: alaleiwang
